title: Implement GitHub issue creation in feedback command
description:
## Description
Define the GitHub issue creation logic in the feedback.md command using the gh CLI.

## Instructions
The feedback.md command should instruct the agent to:

1. **For each issue identified**, create a GitHub issue using:
   \`\`\`bash
   gh issue create --repo hk9890/opencode-coder \
     --title "<descriptive title>" \
     --body "<structured body>"
   \`\`\`

2. **Issue title format**:
   - Short, descriptive summary
   - Include the affected component (e.g., "[bd] command fails with X")
   - Example: "[bd ready] Returns empty when issues exist"

3. **Issue body structure**:
   \`\`\`markdown
   ## Problem
   <Clear description of what went wrong>

   ## Expected Behavior
   <What should have happened>

   ## Actual Behavior
   <What actually happened>

   ## Steps to Reproduce
   <If known, list steps>

   ## Environment
   - Session context: <what was user doing>
   - Commands involved: <list of commands>

   ## Additional Context
   <Error messages, outputs, or relevant info>

   ---
   *Created via /coder/feedback command*
   \`\`\`

4. **One issue per problem** - Do not batch multiple issues into one ticket

5. **Report back** - After creating issues, report:
   - Number of issues created
   - Links to each issue
   - Summary of what was reported

## Target Repository
https://github.com/hk9890/opencode-coder

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gh issue create command is correctly specified
- [ ] Issue format is clear and actionable for fixing
- [ ] One issue per problem (not batched)
- [ ] Feedback includes links to created issues
- [ ] Handles "no issues to report" gracefully
close_reason: GitHub issue creation logic implemented in feedback.md Step 4 - uses gh issue create with heredoc body, one issue per problem, proper title format with component brackets, includes footer marker

title: Fix /dump-session command path — loader requires subdirectory
description:
## Description

Task oc-ll2s instructs creating `knowledge-base/command/dump-session.md` as a flat file to get the command name `/dump-session`. This will NOT work.

The command loader (`src/kb/loaders/commands.ts`) **only reads files inside subdirectories** of `knowledge-base/command/`. The loader iterates directory entries, skips non-directories (`if (!category.isDirectory()) continue`), then reads `.md` files inside each subdirectory. A flat file at `knowledge-base/command/dump-session.md` will be silently ignored.

## Evidence

From `src/kb/loaders/commands.ts` lines 32-41:
```typescript
const categories = await fs.readdir(commandDir, { withFileTypes: true });
for (const category of categories) {
  if (!category.isDirectory()) continue;  // <-- skips flat files
  // ... reads .md files inside the category directory
}
```

## Correct Approach

The file must go in a subdirectory: `knowledge-base/command/coder/dump-session.md`

This means the command name will be `coder/dump-session`, invoked as `/coder/dump-session` (not `/dump-session`).

## Options for Resolution

1. **Accept `/coder/dump-session`** — Place file at `knowledge-base/command/coder/dump-session.md`. Simple and consistent with the loader design. The `coder/` prefix also namespaces it nicely with the coder tool.

2. **Modify the loader** — Update `loadCommands()` to also handle flat `.md` files in the command root. This is a broader change that affects the KB system and is out of scope for this epic.

## Recommendation

Option 1: use `knowledge-base/command/coder/dump-session.md` and accept `/coder/dump-session` as the command name. Update the task description and acceptance criteria accordingly.
close_reason: Acknowledged: command file must be at knowledge-base/command/coder/dump-session.md (not flat). Command name will be /coder/dump-session. Comment added to oc-ll2s.

title: Add Orchestration Principles section to beads-planner-agent
description:
## Description
Add "Orchestration Principles" section to replace/complement any success criteria checklists.

## Instructions
1. Open knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md
2. Add section after "Core Philosophy" (around line 269-277)
3. Insert:

```markdown
## Orchestration Principles

As the primary orchestrator, you coordinate the full workflow:

1. **Parallel Execution** - When multiple tasks are ready and independent, spawn task agents in parallel (single message, multiple tool calls)

2. **Continuous Progress** - After agents complete work, check `bd ready` for newly unblocked tasks and continue

3. **Verification Before Closure** - Always verify gates before closing epics

4. **Complete the Loop** - Don't stop at planning; see the work through to verified completion

5. **User Communication** - Keep user informed of progress, blockers, and completion

**You decide when work is complete** based on:
- Epic objectives achieved
- All critical tasks closed
- Gates verified and passing
- User satisfied with outcome
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Section added after Core Philosophy
- [ ] Lists 5 key orchestration principles
- [ ] Emphasizes agent autonomy in deciding completion
- [ ] Clear and concise
- [ ] Complements existing workflow sections

## Files to Modify
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md (after line ~277)
close_reason: Added Orchestration Principles section after Core Philosophy. Section lists 5 principles (Parallel Execution, Continuous Progress, Verification Before Closure, Complete the Loop, User Communication) plus completion criteria. Flows naturally between existing sections.

title: Fix beads-verify-agent: Require execution evidence and forbid substitution
description:
## Description

Fix the `beads-verify-agent` to prevent it from bypassing explicit verification steps by substituting safer read-only commands. The agent must execute the actual specified steps and provide evidence.

## Problem

The verify agent:
1. Substitutes `--help`, `status`, `list` instead of actually running commands
2. Does "code review" instead of functional testing
3. Claims "verified" without evidence of execution

## Instructions

Edit `knowledge-base/agent/beads-verify-agent.md` to add these sections:

### 1. Add "Verification Integrity Rules" section (after "What You Do NOT Do")

```markdown
## Verification Integrity Rules

**CRITICAL**: These rules are non-negotiable.

### No Substitution Rule

When gate criteria specify "Run X", you MUST run exactly X:

| Criteria Says | You MUST Do | You MUST NOT Do |
|---------------|-------------|-----------------|
| "Run `command`" | Run `command` | Run `command --help` or `command status` |
| "Test workflow end-to-end" | Actually execute the workflow | Code review the workflow |
| "Verify X works" | Execute X and observe result | Infer from reading code |

**If a command has side effects (creates files, opens windows, modifies state), you MUST execute it anyway.** Verification requires observing actual behavior, not theoretical behavior.

### Evidence Requirement

For EVERY verification step, you MUST provide:

1. **Exact command run** - copy/paste the command
2. **Actual output** - copy/paste the result
3. **Observation** - what you concluded from the output

Example:
```
### Step 2: Run `os` - should create session file and start kitty session

**Command**: `os`
**Output**:
```
Starting session 'session:hans' in new window
```
**Observation**: Command ran but checking `os list` shows session not running - FAIL
```

### Code Review is NOT Functional Testing

- Reading code tells you what SHOULD happen
- Functional testing tells you what ACTUALLY happens
- These can differ (bugs exist because of this difference)
- **Never substitute code review for a step that says "Run" or "Test"**

### Risk Acceptance

You may encounter commands that:
- Spawn windows/processes
- Modify files
- Have network effects
- Change system state

**Execute them anyway.** Your job is to verify actual behavior. If you cannot execute a step, report it as UNVERIFIED (not PASS).
```

### 2. Add "Unverifiable Steps" section

```markdown
## Handling Unverifiable Steps

If you genuinely CANNOT execute a verification step:

1. Mark it as `UNVERIFIED` (not PASS, not FAIL)
2. Explain WHY you cannot execute it
3. DO NOT close the gate

Example:
```markdown
- [ ] Step 3: Run `os` twice, verify focus
  - **Status**: UNVERIFIED
  - **Reason**: Cannot spawn GUI windows in this environment
  - **Recommendation**: Requires manual verification by user
```

A gate with ANY unverified steps stays OPEN until a human confirms.
```

### 3. Update Output Format

In the "For Gate Verification" output format section, add evidence requirement:

```markdown
### Verification Evidence

For each criterion that required execution:

**Criterion**: <what was being verified>
**Command**: `<exact command run>`
**Output**:
```
<actual output>
```
**Result**: PASS | FAIL | UNVERIFIED
```

## Files to Modify

- `knowledge-base/agent/beads-verify-agent.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] "Verification Integrity Rules" section added with No Substitution Rule
- [ ] Evidence requirement clearly documented
- [ ] "Code Review is NOT Functional Testing" explicitly stated
- [ ] "Handling Unverifiable Steps" section added
- [ ] Output format requires evidence for each executed step
- [ ] UNVERIFIED status option documented (distinct from PASS/FAIL)

## References

- GitHub Issue #3: https://github.com/hk9890/opencode-coder/issues/3
- Linked bug: oc-xchq
close_reason: Added Verification Integrity Rules section with No Substitution Rule, Evidence Requirement, Code Review distinction, Risk Acceptance guidance; Added Handling Unverifiable Steps section; Updated output format to include Verification Evidence and UNVERIFIED outcome option

title: Audit all commands in ai-resources/commands/ for public vs internal
description:
## Description
Review every command in `ai-resources/commands/` and classify as public (ships to users) vs internal (plugin dev only). Also check if any public commands reference internal tools or paths that won't exist in user projects.

## Instructions
1. Read each command file:
   - `ai-resources/commands/init.md` — public init command
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/doctor.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/dump-session.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/install.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/report-bug.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/status.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder-dev/analyze-logs.md` (352 lines)
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder-dev/fix-bugs.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder-dev/import-tasks.md`
   - `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder-dev/release.md`
2. For each command, determine:
   - **Audience**: user / plugin developer / both
   - **Broken references**: does it reference tools/scripts/paths that only exist in the dev repo?
   - **Recommendation**: keep in ai-resources / move to .opencode / needs rewrite
3. Check if public commands load the opencode-coder skill and whether that works correctly

## Output
Create a structured report as a comment on this issue with a table:
| Command | Audience | Broken Refs | Recommendation |

DO NOT edit any files. Research only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 command files reviewed
- [ ] Each classified by audience
- [ ] Broken references identified
- [ ] Clear recommendations
close_reason: Audit complete. All 10 commands reviewed and classified. Findings added as comment: 4 opencode-coder-dev/ commands are dev-only and misplaced in ai-resources; status.md has 1 broken skill reference; 6 public commands are correctly placed. Identified 3 action items for follow-up.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: test comment

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Audit Findings: ai-resources/commands/ - Public vs Internal Classification

### Summary

All 10 command files reviewed. Key finding: the entire `opencode-coder-dev/` namespace is wrongly in `ai-resources/` — these commands reference dev-repo-only tools and should never reach end users.

---

### Command Audit Table

| Command | Audience | Broken References | Recommendation |
|---------|----------|-------------------|----------------|
| `/init` | Users | None | Keep in ai-resources |
| `/opencode-coder/doctor` | Users | None | Keep in ai-resources |
| `/opencode-coder/dump-session` | Users | Minor: assumes `private/session-dump/` convention | Keep in ai-resources |
| `/opencode-coder/install` | Users | None | Keep in ai-resources |
| `/opencode-coder/report-bug` | Users | None | Keep in ai-resources |
| `/opencode-coder/status` | Users | Line 88: references `references/status-checks-reference.md` but actual file is `references/status-health.md` (broken!) | Fix broken ref, keep in ai-resources |
| `/opencode-coder-dev/analyze-logs` | Plugin devs ONLY | `bun run scripts/log-analyzer` (dev repo only), self-refs as `/internal/analyze-logs` (stale path) | Move to .opencode/commands/ |
| `/opencode-coder-dev/fix-bugs` | Plugin devs ONLY | Cross-ref to `/internal/import-tasks` (stale path) | Move to .opencode/commands/ |
| `/opencode-coder-dev/import-tasks` | Plugin devs ONLY | Cross-ref to `/internal/fix-bugs` (stale path) | Move to .opencode/commands/ |
| `/opencode-coder-dev/release` | Plugin devs ONLY | References `docs/RELEASING.md` (dev repo only) | Move to .opencode/commands/ |

---

### Detailed Findings

#### PUBLIC commands (opencode-coder/ namespace) - CORRECT location

**`/init`** — Well-structured, no broken refs. Loads the `opencode-coder` skill which ships in ai-resources. Good user-facing command.

**`/opencode-coder/doctor`** — Clean. Delegates to `opencode-coder` skill (exists in ai-resources). Correct for all users.

**`/opencode-coder/install`** — Clean. Loads `opencode-coder` skill for guidance. Appropriate for all users.

**`/opencode-coder/report-bug`** — Delegates to `opencode-coder` skill. Uses `gh issue create --repo hk9890/opencode-coder` — appropriate for users reporting plugin bugs.

**`/opencode-coder/dump-session`** — Standalone, no skill dependency. Minor: assumes `private/session-dump/` path convention but acceptable as default output location.

**`/opencode-coder/status`** — Works well for direct bash-based status checks. ONE BROKEN REFERENCE: line 88 says `references/status-checks-reference.md` but the actual file in the opencode-coder skill is `references/status-health.md`. This will confuse agents trying to follow the reference.

#### INTERNAL/DEV commands (opencode-coder-dev/ namespace) - WRONG location

**`/opencode-coder-dev/analyze-logs`** — Entirely dev-only. References:
- `bun run scripts/log-analyzer` (script only exists in plugin source repo at `scripts/log-analyzer/`)
- Self-references as `/internal/analyze-logs` in 2 places (stale path — should be `/opencode-coder-dev/analyze-logs`)
- Command is useless and misleading for end users

**`/opencode-coder-dev/fix-bugs`** — Dev workflow for managing plugin's own GitHub issues. Cross-ref to `/internal/import-tasks` (stale path). No value for end users.

**`/opencode-coder-dev/import-tasks`** — Dev workflow for importing plugin issues from GitHub. Cross-ref to `/internal/fix-bugs` (stale path). No value for end users.

**`/opencode-coder-dev/release`** — Explicitly references `docs/RELEASING.md` which only exists in the plugin dev repo. End users have no such file.

---

### Skill Dependency Analysis

Public commands loading `opencode-coder` skill: init.md, doctor.md, install.md, report-bug.md, status.md

The `opencode-coder` skill DOES exist in `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/` — so this dependency is VALID for public users.

Dev commands: fix-bugs and import-tasks load `task-sync` skill (valid), release loads `github-releases` skill (valid). But all opencode-coder-dev/ commands have dev-context that makes them inappropriate for end users.

---

### Additional Observations

1. **Stale `/internal/` path refs**: These commands previously lived in an `internal/` namespace. Internal cross-references (e.g. in analyze-logs: `/internal/analyze-logs`, fix-bugs: `/internal/import-tasks`) were never updated to match the current `opencode-coder-dev/` folder name.

2. **npm delivery**: `package.json` `files` field only includes `dist/`. The `ai-resources/` directory is distributed via `aimgr` separately. But AGENTS.md states ai-resources is for "Commands for plugin management (install, init, doctor)" — the opencode-coder-dev/ workflow commands clearly don't fit.

3. **`opencode-coder-dev/` name is confusing**: Users installing via aimgr would see commands like `/opencode-coder-dev/release` which would try to release the plugin itself (not their project) and reference non-existent files.

---

### Recommended Action Items

1. Move `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder-dev/` → `.opencode/commands/` (or integrate into forthcoming opencode-coder-dev skill per epic oc-ts0z)
2. Fix broken ref in `status.md` line 88: `status-checks-reference.md` → `status-health.md`
3. Update stale `/internal/*` cross-references in dev commands to use `/opencode-coder-dev/*`
4. These findings feed into epic oc-ts0z (Create opencode-coder-dev skill and separate internal from public content)

title: Update README.md - beads is optional, add stealth mode info
description:
## Description
Update README.md to clarify that beads is optional, not required. The plugin can work without beads. When beads is used, there are two modes: stealth (local-only, recommended) and team (git-synced).

## Instructions
1. Edit README.md at project root
2. Make the following changes:

### Features Section
Update "Beads Integration" to show it's optional:
- **Beads Integration (Optional)** - Local-first issue tracking with stealth mode (local-only) or team mode (git-synced)

### Prerequisites
- Remove the implication that beads is required
- Keep Node.js, Bun/npm, OpenCode CLI as required

### Installation
1. Remove "Install beads CLI" as a required step
2. Move beads installation to the "Optional: Beads Setup" section or similar
3. Keep plugin config step
4. Keep /coder/install and /coder/init steps

### Add "Using with Beads" Section (after Quick Start or as part of it)
Explain the two modes:

**Stealth Mode (Recommended)**
- Beads files stay local to your machine
- Won't affect git history or other team members
- Perfect for: personal use, OSS contributions, teams not using beads yet
- Initialized via: `bd init --stealth` (or `/coder/init` default)

**Team Mode**
- Beads files are committed and synced via git
- Enables multi-device sync and team collaboration
- Perfect for: teams adopting beads together
- Initialized via: `bd init`

### Quick Start
- Update to show plugin works without beads
- Optionally show beads commands as "if using beads"

## Files to Modify
- README.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Beads shown as optional feature, not required
- [ ] Stealth mode and team mode explained
- [ ] Installation doesn't require beads CLI first
- [ ] Plugin can be used without beads (basic functionality)
- [ ] bd/* commands clearly shown as "for beads users"
close_reason: Updated README.md: beads shown as optional, added stealth/team mode section, restructured installation to remove beads CLI as requirement, added 'Using with Beads' section, bd/* commands noted as requiring beads

title: Audit all opencode-coder skill references for public vs internal content
description:
## Description
Review every file in `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/` and classify content as public (useful to any user) vs internal (only useful for plugin developers).

## Instructions
1. Read each reference file in `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/`:
   - `debugging-logs.md` (680 lines) — KNOWN MIXED: log analyzer section is internal
   - `bug-reporting.md` (389 lines) — KNOWN MIXED: likely has internal patterns
   - `agents-md-template.md` (455 lines)
   - `troubleshooting-patterns.md` (452 lines)
   - `installation-setup.md` (271 lines)
   - `planning.md` (269 lines)
   - `status-health.md` (152 lines)
2. For each file, classify every major section as:
   - **Public** — useful for any user of the plugin
   - **Internal** — only useful for plugin developers
   - **Mixed** — needs splitting
3. Also check `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/assets/` and `scripts/`
4. Note which content should move to the dev skill, which should stay, and which should be deleted/compressed

## Output
Create a structured report as a comment on this issue with:
- File-by-file classification
- Recommended action per file (keep/move/split/compress)
- Estimated line savings for the public skill

DO NOT edit any files. Research only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 reference files reviewed
- [ ] Assets and scripts reviewed
- [ ] Each file has a clear public/internal/mixed classification
- [ ] Recommendations for what goes where
close_reason: Audit complete. All 7 reference files + assets + scripts reviewed. Findings: agents-md-template.md (455 lines) should move entirely to dev skill; debugging-logs.md needs split at line 213 (468 lines internal); bug-reporting.md and troubleshooting-patterns.md need minor compression (~29 lines combined). Total estimated savings: ~952 lines (~42%). Three bugs found in troubleshooting-patterns.md and debugging-logs.md.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Audit Findings: opencode-coder Skill References

Date: 2026-02-28
Total current lines: ~2,268. Estimated after cleanup: ~1,316 (~42% reduction).

### Summary by File

**KEEP as-is (fully public):**
- installation-setup.md (271 lines) - all public
- planning.md (269 lines) - all public
- status-health.md (152 lines) - all public
- assets/bug-report-template.md (61 lines) - all public
- scripts/collect-system-info.sh (40 lines) - all public
- SKILL.md (20 lines) - all public

**SPLIT (move internal section to dev skill):**
- debugging-logs.md (680 lines): Lines 34-210 are PUBLIC (debug logging, log locations, grep commands). Lines 213-680 are INTERNAL (the log analyzer tool that requires bun runtime + dev repo checkout). The file itself says on line 238 it is "primarily for plugin developers". Savings: ~468 lines.

**MOVE entirely to dev skill:**
- agents-md-template.md (455 lines): Entirely INTERNAL. This is AI-to-AI instructions for how the AI should generate AGENTS.md during /init. Users never read this; it is implementation guidance for the plugin's AI behavior. 100% internal content.

**COMPRESS (minor fixes only):**
- bug-reporting.md (389 lines): Mostly public. Two internal path issues: (1) line 85 references ./scripts/collect-system-info.sh assuming dev repo checkout, (2) line 232 uses `cp ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/assets/...` which is a dev repo path. ~15 line savings if those subsections are removed/rewritten.
- troubleshooting-patterns.md (452 lines): Mostly public. Only lines 37-50 (Quick Search Patterns) are internal -- they use `grep -n ... references/troubleshooting-patterns.md` assuming skill files are on disk at a known path. ~14 line savings.

### Content for Dev Skill

1. debugging-logs.md lines 213-680: Log analyzer tool docs (bun run scripts/log-analyzer)
2. agents-md-template.md entire file: AGENTS.md generation instructions for AI
3. bug-reporting.md lines 82-97: Automated collection via ./scripts/collect-system-info.sh path
4. bug-reporting.md lines 228-242: `cp ai-resources/...` dev repo path
5. troubleshooting-patterns.md lines 37-50: Quick Search Patterns (grep assumes file is on disk)

### Bugs Found During Audit

1. troubleshooting-patterns.md line 287: References `analyze-opencode-logs` skill that does not exist
2. troubleshooting-patterns.md line 440: References "#reporting-issues section in SKILL.md" -- SKILL.md has no such section
3. debugging-logs.md line 238: Note says "primarily for plugin developers" but the content is in the public skill anyway

### Line Savings Estimate

| File | Action | Savings |
|------|--------|---------|
| debugging-logs.md | SPLIT | ~468 lines |
| agents-md-template.md | MOVE | ~455 lines |
| bug-reporting.md | COMPRESS | ~15 lines |
| troubleshooting-patterns.md | COMPRESS | ~14 lines |
| Total | | ~952 lines |

title: Implement bb-api.sh core API library
description:
## Description
Create the core Bash library with reusable functions for all Bitbucket API operations. This is the foundation all other scripts depend on.

## Instructions
1. Create `ai-resources/skills/bitbucket-pr/scripts/bb-api.sh` with:

   **Configuration functions:**
   - `bb_init()` — Check token, detect project/repo from git remote
   - `bb_check_token()` — Verify BITBUCKET_TOKEN is set

   **Read operations:**
   - `bb_get_pr()` — Get PR details
   - `bb_get_pr_changes()` — Get list of changed files
   - `bb_get_pr_commits()` — Get commits in PR
   - `bb_get_pr_comments()` — Get all comments
   - `bb_get_pr_tasks()` — Get all tasks
   - `bb_get_pr_diff()` — Get full diff or file-specific diff

   **Write operations:**
   - `bb_create_pr()` — Create new PR
   - `bb_update_pr()` — Update PR title/description
   - `bb_add_comment()` — Add general comment
   - `bb_add_file_comment()` — Add inline comment (line-specific)
   - `bb_create_task()` — Create task
   - `bb_resolve_task()` — Mark task as resolved
   - `bb_approve_pr()` — Approve PR

   **Helper functions:**
   - `bb_parse_pr_url()` — Extract project/repo/pr from URL
   - `bb_get_current_branch()` — Get current git branch
   - `bb_detect_target_branch()` — Detect target (main/master/develop)
   - `bb_extract_repo_info()` — Get project/repo from git remote

2. Implementation requirements:
   - Use `curl` for API calls
   - Use `jq` for JSON parsing
   - Consistent error handling (return codes + stderr messages)
   - All functions output structured data (JSON or tab-separated)
   - Base URL: `https://bitbucket.lab.dynatrace.org/rest/api/1.0`
   - Authentication: `Authorization: Bearer $BITBUCKET_TOKEN`

3. Test each function manually:
   ```bash
   export BITBUCKET_TOKEN='your_token'
   source scripts/bb-api.sh
   bb_get_pr PFS copilot-skills 2639
   ```

## Files to Create
- ai-resources/skills/bitbucket-pr/scripts/bb-api.sh

## Reference
- docs/brainstorming/bitbucket-pr-skill-proposal.md (full function list and API details)
- docs/brainstorming/test-bitbucket.sh (test script example)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bb-api.sh created with all functions listed above
- [ ] All read operations tested and working against real Bitbucket
- [ ] All write operations implemented
- [ ] Error handling works (clear messages for missing token, invalid PR, etc.)
- [ ] Functions return structured, parseable data
- [ ] Code is well-commented
close_reason: Implemented bb-api.sh with all 19 functions: 3 internal helpers (_bb_curl, _bb_curl_text, _bb_paginate), 2 config functions (bb_init, bb_check_token), 4 helper functions (bb_parse_pr_url, bb_get_current_branch, bb_detect_target_branch, bb_extract_repo_info), 6 read operations (bb_get_pr, bb_get_pr_changes, bb_get_pr_commits, bb_get_pr_comments, bb_get_pr_tasks, bb_get_pr_diff), and 7 write operations (bb_create_pr, bb_update_pr, bb_add_comment, bb_add_file_comment, bb_create_task, bb_resolve_task, bb_approve_pr). All jq payloads verified valid. Bash syntax clean. TODO comment added in bb_init for future check-prerequisites.sh delegation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: REFINEMENT: bb_init() should source/call check-prerequisites.sh internally, so any script that calls bb_init() automatically validates the environment. This replaces having each script do its own token/jq checks.

description:

title: Test /init on different project types
description:

(3) Consider adding a specific test for the 'no skills installed' edge case. Priority P2 is appropriate for testing.

title: Fix plugin installation path in status-health.md (GitHub #6)
description:
## Description

Fix incorrect npm-based plugin paths in status-health.md to use correct OpenCode cache directory paths.

## Instructions

1. Open `.opencode/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md`

2. Find and replace plugin path pattern (lines 118-128):
   - **OLD**: `PLUGIN_PATH="$(npm root -g)/opencode-coder"`
   - **NEW**: `PLUGIN_PATH="$HOME/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder"`

3. Fix version check commands section (lines 250-257):
   - **Remove**:
     ```bash
     npm list -g opencode-coder
     node -p "require('$(npm root -g)/opencode-coder/package.json').version"
     npm outdated -g beads opencode-coder
     ```
   - **Replace with**:
     ```bash
     # Check installed OpenCode plugins
     ls -la ~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/
     
     # Plugin version
     cat ~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder/package.json | grep version
     
     # Or with node
     node -p "require('$HOME/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder/package.json').version"
     
     # Check configured plugins
     cat ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json | grep -A5 '"plugin"'
     ```

4. Fix update instructions section (lines 280-291):
   - **Remove**:
     ```bash
     npm list -g opencode-coder
     npm update -g opencode-coder
     npm install -g opencode-coder@1.2.0
     ```
   - **Replace with**:
     ```bash
     # Plugins are automatically managed by OpenCode
     # To use a different version, update opencode.json:
     # "plugin": ["@hk9890/opencode-coder@1.2.0"]
     
     # Then restart OpenCode to trigger reinstallation
     # Or clear cache and restart:
     rm -rf ~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder
     # OpenCode will reinstall on next startup
     ```

5. Fix dependency troubleshooting section (lines 296-307):
   - **OLD**: `node -p "require('$(npm root -g)/opencode-coder/package.json').peerDependencies"`
   - **NEW**: `node -p "require('$HOME/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder/package.json').peerDependencies"`
   - **Remove**: `npm update -g beads opencode-coder`
   - **Add note**: "Plugin updates are managed by OpenCode based on opencode.json"

## Files to Modify

- `.opencode/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md` - Update all plugin paths and commands

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All `$(npm root -g)/opencode-coder` replaced with `~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder` or `$HOME/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder`

title: Implement duplicate detection and beads search
description:
## Description
Add instructions to analyze-logs.md for checking if beads issues already exist for detected errors.

## Instructions
The analyze-logs.md command should instruct the agent to:

1. **For each unique error found**, search existing beads issues:
   \`\`\`bash
   bd search "<error message or key phrase>"
   \`\`\`

2. **Match criteria** - Consider an issue a duplicate if:
   - Title contains the error message or key identifiers
   - Description mentions the same service/component
   - Error pattern matches (not just exact string match)

3. **Classification of matches**:
   - **Exact match**: Issue already exists for this exact error
   - **Similar match**: Related issue exists, may need update
   - **No match**: New error, needs new bug created

4. **Handle existing issues**:
   - If exact match exists and is **open**: Note as duplicate, skip creation
   - If exact match exists and is **closed**: May need to reopen or create new
   - If similar match: Consider updating with new information

5. **Track decisions** for reporting:
   - Errors that will get new bugs
   - Errors that match existing bugs
   - Errors that need review (unclear match)

## Key Commands
- \`bd search "<query>"\` - Search issues by text
- \`bd show <id>\` - Get full issue details to check match
- \`bd list --status=open\` - List open issues only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search instructions are clear
- [ ] Match criteria is well-defined
- [ ] Handles closed vs open duplicates
- [ ] Tracking of decisions for report
- [ ] Conservative approach (when in doubt, don't create duplicate)
close_reason: Implemented Step 3 (duplicate detection) with bd search commands and match criteria

title: Fix version.test.ts CI failures
description:
## Description
The `getVersionInfo()` function returns "unknown" in CI because path resolution fails before the build step. Tests expect valid semver but get fallback value.

## Root Cause
In `src/core/version.ts`, the function tries these paths:
- `dist/../package.json` (for built code)
- `src/core/../../package.json` (for development)

In CI, tests run from the `tests/` directory context, so neither path works.

## Instructions
1. Fix the path resolution in `src/core/version.ts`:
   - Add more path candidates that work in test context
   - Try `process.cwd() + '/package.json'`
   - Try searching up directory tree until package.json found
2. Alternatively, mock the version in tests for CI environment
3. Run tests locally: `bun test tests/unit/version.test.ts`
4. Verify fix in CI-like environment if possible

## Files to Modify
- src/core/version.ts - improve path resolution
- OR tests/unit/version.test.ts - mock version for tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] version.test.ts passes locally
- [ ] Tests will pass in CI (verify logic)
- [ ] getVersionInfo() returns valid semver, not "unknown"
- [ ] No breaking changes to the version API
close_reason: Fixed by oc-go0b - global mocking removed, tests now work correctly

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: INVESTIGATION: Test passes locally but fails in CI with 'unknown' version. The getVersionInfo() function tries these paths: dist/../package.json and src/core/../../package.json. Both should work after the build step runs (which happens BEFORE tests in CI). Need to add more path candidates or add debug logging to see which path is being tried in CI environment.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: skill-service.test.ts uses mock.module('fs/promises') which globally mocks fs for ALL tests. The mockAccess throws errors, preventing version.ts from finding package.json. Fix: Either isolate skill-service.test.ts in a separate test run, OR refactor to use dependency injection instead of module mocking.

title: Stealth Mode Default for /coder/init
description:
## Description
Update the `/coder/init` command to ask users how they want to initialize beads, defaulting to stealth mode. This enables using beads in repos where other team members don't use beads (no commits/pushes of beads files).

## Context
- Beads already has full stealth mode support (`bd init --stealth`)
- The plugin's context injection via `bd prime` already adapts to the mode
- This is a one-time setup per project, so interactive prompt is appropriate

## Goals
- User chooses between "stealth" (local only) and "team" (git synced) mode during init
- Stealth mode is the default/recommended option
- Existing repos already using team mode are unaffected
- No changes needed to context injection (bd prime handles this)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `/coder/init` prompts user for mode selection
- [ ] Stealth mode uses `bd init --stealth`
- [ ] Team mode uses `bd init` (current behavior)
- [ ] Clear explanation of each mode shown to user
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: All tasks completed, gate passed. /coder/init now prompts for stealth vs team mode, defaulting to stealth.

title: Implement Step 3: Import GitHub issues to beads
description:
## Description
Implement the import phase that brings open GitHub issues into beads.

## Instructions
1. Fetch open GitHub issues: `gh issue list --repo $REPO --state open --json number,title,body,labels,createdAt --limit 100`
2. For each issue, check if already exists in beads:
   - Use `bd list --json` and check for `github:<number>` label or title pattern
3. For new issues, create beads issue:
   - Use `bd create` with `--body-file -` for multi-line content
   - Add labels: `source:external`, `github:<number>`
   - Map GitHub priority labels to beads priorities (P0-P4)
   - Include GitHub metadata in body
4. Track and report import statistics

## Priority Mapping
- critical, P0 → P0 (0)
- high, P1 → P1 (1)  
- medium, P2 → P2 (2) [default]
- low, P3 → P3 (3)
- backlog, P4 → P4 (4)

## Output Format
Table showing:
- GitHub # | Beads ID | Title
- Count of imported vs skipped

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fetches all open GitHub issues
- [ ] Checks for existing beads (no duplicates)
- [ ] Creates beads with correct labels and priority
- [ ] Reports import statistics
- [ ] Handles API errors gracefully

## Files to Modify
- Command file
close_reason: Implemented Step 3 with comprehensive import workflow. Added duplicate detection, priority mapping, error handling, and detailed reporting. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Implement system-info tool in plugin
description:
## Description
Implement a custom OpenCode tool `system_info` that provides runtime information about the opencode-coder plugin. This tool will be callable by Claude and skills to get structured JSON data about plugin status, integrations, and health.

## Goals
- Create tool in `src/system-info/` following plugin structure
- Register tool via plugin's `tool` hook
- Return minimal JSON (plugin version + beads status)
- Enable skills to call tool and use output
- Provide foundation for future expansion

## Architecture

### Directory Structure
```
src/system-info/
  index.ts           # Public API
  tool.ts            # Tool definition and registration
  collectors/        # Data collection modules
    plugin.ts        # Plugin info collector
    beads.ts         # Beads info collector
  types.ts           # TypeScript types for output
```

### Integration Points
- Plugin registers tool in `src/index.ts` via `tool` hook
- Tool has access to all services (BeadsService, etc.)
- Tool uses existing detectors/contexts

## Minimal Scope (Phase 1)

### Output Structure
```json
{
  "metadata": {
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "generatedAt": "2026-01-25T10:30:00.000Z"
  },
  "plugin": {
    "name": "@hk9890/opencode-coder",
    "version": "0.21.0",
    "active": true
  },
  "integrations": {
    "beads": {
      "enabled": true,
      "cliVersion": "0.49.0",
      "projectInitialized": true,
      "stats": {
        "openIssues": 4,
        "readyToWork": 1
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Tool Interface
- **Name**: `system_info`
- **Args**: None (for minimal version)
- **Returns**: JSON string
- **Context**: Has access to sessionID, messageID, agent

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Tool implemented in `src/system-info/`
- [ ] Tool registered in plugin
- [ ] Skills can call tool successfully
- [ ] Returns valid JSON with plugin + beads info
- [ ] Error handling (null values when commands missing)
- [ ] Tests pass (if applicable)
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Future Expansions (NOT in Phase 1)
- Git integration status
- GitHub integration status  
- Health checks
- Knowledge base stats
- Session information
- Optional section filtering

## Notes
- Follow existing plugin patterns (service structure)
- Use existing BeadsService for beads detection
- Error handling: Option A (null values, not errors)
- Tool must be part of plugin core (not `.opencode/tools/`)
close_reason: All tasks complete and verified. Success criteria met: (1) Tool implemented in src/system-info/ with proper structure, (2) Tool registered in plugin via tool hook, (3) Skills can call tool successfully verified by oc-47w6, (4) Returns valid JSON with plugin + beads info, (5) Error handling with null values implemented, (6) Tests passed. System-info tool is fully functional and tested.

7. **Remove "Details: [link]" lines** - agents can find references if needed
8. **Condense Gate Template** - shorter checklist

## Keep These (Critical)

- STOP: Prerequisites Check (first one)
- ALL Phases Are REQUIRED section
- MANDATORY: Create Release Structure with bd commands
- Example Release Structure diagram
- ABSOLUTE RULE about test failures (shorter version)

- Basic task template structure (but shorter)

title: Fix incorrect heredoc syntax in sync-github-issues.md
description:
## Description

The sync-github-issues.md command documentation uses incorrect heredoc syntax that causes stdin content to be silently ignored.

## Root Cause

Lines 76-93 use:
```bash
bd create --title="..." --body-file - <<'EOF'
```

This is WRONG. The heredoc at the end doesn't pipe to stdin properly.

## Fix Required

Change to the correct pipe-from-cat syntax:
```bash
cat << 'EOF' | bd create --title="..." --body-file -
```

## Files to Modify

- knowledge-base/command/coder/sync-github-issues.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All bd create examples use correct `cat << 'EOF' | bd create ... --body-file -` syntax
- [ ] No instances of `--body-file - <<'EOF'` pattern remain

## Related

Fixes GitHub #1 (oc-ntp)
close_reason: Fixed heredoc syntax: changed --body-file - <<'EOF' to cat << 'EOF' | bd ... --body-file -

title: Guard default_agent on beads agent availability
description:
## What

The `processConfig` method unconditionally sets `default_agent = "orchestrator"` when beads is detected. This breaks if the user has .beads/ but hasn't installed the beads agents via aimgr.

## How

- In `processConfig()`, check if the orchestrator agent is actually available before setting it as default
- Options for detection:
  a. Check if orchestrator.md exists in the expected agent path
  b. Check the opencode config for registered agents
  c. Simply don't set default_agent at all — let the user configure it
- Consider whether setting default_agent from a plugin is appropriate at all

## Acceptance Criteria

- Setting default_agent does not break sessions when beads agents are not installed
- If agents are not available, default_agent is not modified
- TypeScript compiles cleanly

## Files

- `src/service/beads-service.ts` (processConfig method)

title: Audit knowledge-base for project-specific content
description:
## Description

The `knowledge-base/` folder is released with the npm package and accessible to ALL systems using this plugin. We've discovered that project-specific content was incorrectly placed there (release.md for this plugin's release workflow).

This task is to audit ALL content in `knowledge-base/` and ensure nothing project-specific remains.

## Background

Agent made this mistake twice, placing project-specific commands in the released knowledge-base instead of `.opencode/`. Need a thorough audit.

## Instructions

1. Review every file in `knowledge-base/`:
   - `knowledge-base/agent/` - should only contain generic agents
   - `knowledge-base/command/bd/` - should only contain generic bd commands
   - `knowledge-base/command/coder/` - should only contain generic coder commands
   - `knowledge-base/command/internal/` - review if this should even exist in knowledge-base
   - `knowledge-base/documenation/` - should only contain generic docs

2. For each file, ask: "Would this be useful to ANY project using this plugin?"
   - YES → Keep in knowledge-base
   - NO → Move to .opencode/ or delete

3. Pay special attention to:
   - References to `hk9890/opencode-coder` repository
   - References to this plugin's release workflow
   - Commands that only make sense for plugin development
   - Any hardcoded paths or repo-specific configuration

## Suspicious Items to Check

- `knowledge-base/command/internal/coder-sync.md` - is this generic or project-specific?
- Any files mentioning GitHub Actions workflows specific to this repo
- Any files with hardcoded repo names

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All files in knowledge-base reviewed
- [ ] Any project-specific content moved to .opencode/ or deleted
- [ ] Report generated listing what was found and any changes made
close_reason: Audited all 47 files in knowledge-base/. Found 1 project-specific file (feedback.md) with hardcoded repo hk9890/opencode-coder. Moved it to .opencode/command/coder/feedback.md. All other files verified as generic.

title: Add permission configuration to beads-planner-agent frontmatter
description:
## Description
Update the frontmatter of beads-planner-agent.md to add permission configuration that enables the question tool and enforces read-only mode for code.

## Instructions
1. Open `knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md`
2. Update the frontmatter (lines 1-4) to include permission config:
   ```yaml
   ---
   description: Planning agent that designs work into beads issues and orchestrates execution
   mode: primary
   permission:
     question: allow
     edit:
       "*": deny
     bash: allow
   ---
   ```

## Rationale
- `question: allow` - Enables the question tool (mirrors plan agent)
- `edit: {"*": "deny"}` - Explicitly prevents code editing (enforces read-only)
- `bash: allow` - Allows CLI commands like bd, git (BeadsService auto-approves bd *)

## Files to Modify
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md (lines 1-4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Frontmatter includes permission section
- [ ] question: allow is set
- [ ] edit is set to deny all
- [ ] bash: allow is set
- [ ] File remains valid markdown with proper frontmatter syntax
close_reason: Added permission configuration to frontmatter: question tool enabled, edit denied for all files, bash allowed

title: system-info: Improve bd version parsing
description:
The design parses bd version with `.split(' ')[2]` which is fragile. Should use regex or better parsing.

Example:
```typescript
const versionMatch = versionOutput.match(/bd\s+version\s+([\d.]+)/);
const cliVersion = versionMatch ? versionMatch[1] : null;
```

This handles format variations and returns null if parsing fails.
close_reason: Updated design in task oc-yhed to use regex for version parsing. Changed from fragile .split(' ')[2] to robust versionOutput.match(/bd\s+version\s+([\d.]+)/) pattern that handles format variations and returns null on parsing failure.

title: Create playground folder on session start
description:

     const sessionID = output.message.sessionID;
     
     // Create playground folder for this session
     const playgroundPath = await this.playgroundService.getOrCreatePlayground(sessionID);
     this.logger.info(`Playground ready: ${playgroundPath}`);
     ```

2. **Update** `src/service/beads-service.ts` method `processEvent()` (around line 223-239):
   - After getting sessionID from compaction event:
     ```typescript

     await this.playgroundService.getOrCreatePlayground(sessionID);
     ```

3. **Add logging** for debugging:

   - Log any errors from playground service

## Files to Modify

## Dependencies

Updated processEvent to ensure playground exists after session compaction. Build successful. Playground folders now automatically created for each session with unique paths.

title: Log Analysis and Beads Bug Creation
description:
## Description
Create an internal slash command that uses the existing log-analyzer scripts to find errors and warnings in OpenCode logs, then automatically creates or updates beads bugs for any issues found.

## Goals
- Provide a `/internal/analyze-logs` slash command for internal use
- Leverage existing `scripts/log-analyzer/` to parse and filter logs
- Filter for ERROR and WARN level logs
- Check if beads issues already exist for each error (avoid duplicates)
- Create new bugs for new errors, update existing ones if needed
- Streamline the process of capturing issues from production/development logs

## Log Analyzer Scripts
Location: `scripts/log-analyzer/`
- `index.ts` - CLI entry point with filter options
- `parser.ts` - Parses log lines into structured LogLine objects
- `filters.ts` - Filters logs by level, session, pid, service
- `discovery.ts` - Discovers log directory, lists files, finds sessions/processes
- `types.ts` - TypeScript types for LogLine, FilterOptions, etc.

Key Usage:
\`\`\`bash
# List sessions
bun run scripts/log-analyzer list sessions

# Filter by session and level (errors/warnings only)
bun run scripts/log-analyzer --session=ses_xxx --level=WARN,ERROR

# Get JSON output for parsing
bun run scripts/log-analyzer --session=ses_xxx --level=ERROR --json
\`\`\`

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `/internal/analyze-logs` command exists and works
- [ ] Correctly finds and parses errors/warnings from logs
- [ ] Checks for duplicate issues before creating
- [ ] Creates actionable bug reports in beads
- [ ] Updates existing bugs if new information found
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: Epic complete: All child tasks closed, acceptance gate oc-ciq verified and closed. /internal/analyze-logs command fully implemented.

title: Release v0.30.0
description: Complete GitHub release workflow for version 0.30.0
close_reason: Release v0.30.0 fully verified: tag pushed, GitHub release published, npm package live at 0.30.0

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Double version bump conflict between oc-7xu3.3 and oc-7xu3.4. The release.yml workflow already handles version bump, commit, tag, and push (lines 71-99). Doing a manual bump in oc-7xu3.3 first is redundant and creates a race condition window — if any commit lands on main between the manual push and the workflow's push, the workflow fails with a non-fast-forward error. RELEASING.md documents the automated path as 'Recommended' and manual as 'Fallback'. This plan mixes both. Action: Delete oc-7xu3.3 entirely. Let the workflow handle it.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: Release notes (oc-7xu3.5) are sequenced AFTER the release creation (oc-7xu3.4), but oc-7xu3.4 requires release notes as input to gh workflow run. The workaround of using 'gh release edit' after the fact leaves the git tag annotation (baked into the tag object at line 95-97 of release.yml) with incomplete notes permanently. Action: Move oc-7xu3.5 before oc-7xu3.4 in the dependency chain.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: 7 sequential tasks is over-engineered for a 9-commit minor release. oc-7xu3.1 (quality gates) is redundant since the workflow re-runs them. oc-7xu3.3 should be deleted (see other comment). oc-7xu3.2 and oc-7xu3.5 are both documentation tasks that should be merged. oc-7xu3.7 is just 'bd update --status=closed' which doesn't need a tracked task. Suggested simplification: 3 tasks — (1) Prepare CHANGELOG + release notes, (2) Trigger workflow + monitor, (3) Verify + close.

Version bump type (minor) is correct — there are 4 feature commits. CHANGELOG.md currently has an empty [Unreleased] section that needs to be filled. This task correctly identifies that.

title: Version Bump to v0.30.0
description:
## Version Bump to v0.30.0

Update version number in package.json.

### Steps
1. Update version in package.json to 0.30.0
2. Commit with message: "release: v0.30.0"
3. Push to origin/main
4. Verify clean tree

### Files to Update
- `package.json` — version field (only version file in this project)

### Version Update Command
```bash
bun -e "
  const pkg = await Bun.file(\"package.json\").json();
  pkg.version = \"0.30.0\";
  await Bun.write(\"package.json\", JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2) + \"\n\");
"
git add package.json
git commit -m "release: v0.30.0"
git push origin main
```

### STOP if ANY fails
Report to user, do NOT proceed to next phase.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review: This task should be DELETED. The release.yml workflow already handles version bump (lines 71-77), commit (lines 84-91), tag creation (lines 93-99), and push. Doing it manually first is the 'Fallback' path per RELEASING.md — mixing it with the automated path in oc-7xu3.4 creates a race condition. The workflow already handles the case where version is already bumped (git diff --cached --quiet check), but it's still unnecessary complexity and risk.

title: Trigger release workflow for v0.30.0
description:
## Trigger GitHub Actions Release Workflow

The workflow handles: version bump in package.json, git tag, npm publish to GitHub Packages, and GitHub release creation.

### Pre-flight
```bash
git status  # must be clean
gh run list --limit 1  # CI green
```

### Trigger
```bash
gh workflow run release.yml \
  -f version="0.30.0" \
  -f release_notes="<paste release notes prepared in oc-7xu3.2>"
```

### Monitor
```bash
gh run watch
```

The workflow runs: typecheck → build → unit tests → integration tests → version bump → tag → publish → release.

### STOP if workflow fails

title: Cleanup Release Tasks
description:
## Cleanup: Mark release tasks for compaction

Close the epic and mark all tasks with label for future compaction.

### Steps
```bash
# Close the epic
bd update $PARENT --status=closed

# Mark epic and all tasks for compaction
bd update $PARENT --add-label=marked-for-compaction
for task_id in $QUALITY $DOCS $BUMP $RELEASE $NOTES $VERIFY; do
  bd update $task_id --add-label=marked-for-compaction
done

# Mark cleanup task itself
bd update $CLEANUP --add-label=marked-for-compaction
```

This keeps the release history for analysis. Later, these can be compacted into a single summary task.

title: Design and plan task-sync skill structure
description:
## Description

Plan the structure and content organization for the generic `task-sync` skill following Agent Skills specification and skill-creator principles.

## Instructions

1. **Define SKILL.md structure** (~150-200 lines):
   - Frontmatter: name, description with clear triggers
   - Decision tree: Detect sync direction, detect backend, load backend skill
   - High-level workflows (NOT detailed steps - those go in references/)
   - Safety principles and conflict resolution guidelines
   - Metadata conventions (label patterns)

2. **Plan references/ directory**:
   - `import-workflow.md` - Detailed import steps (external → beads)
   - `export-workflow.md` - Detailed export steps (beads → external)
   - `bidirectional-workflow.md` - Combined workflow with conflict handling
   - `safety-guidelines.md` - Conflict resolution, user confirmation patterns

3. **Document skill purpose**:
   - Orchestrator that delegates to backend skills
   - System-agnostic terminology
   - Progressive disclosure: SKILL.md = workflow, references/ = details

4. **Create design document** outlining:
   - SKILL.md sections and approximate line counts
   - Each reference file's purpose and key content
   - How skill invocation works (both direct and via backends)
   - Example skill description (for YAML frontmatter)

## Files to Create

- Design document (markdown) with complete skill structure plan

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clear SKILL.md outline (sections, line estimates)
- [ ] All reference files identified with purpose
- [ ] Skill description written (for frontmatter)
- [ ] Progressive disclosure pattern applied correctly
- [ ] Design follows skill-creator principles
- [ ] Structure matches fix-documentation pattern

## Notes

Reference the `fix-documentation` skill structure (`ai-resources/skills/fix-documentation/SKILL.md`) as a pattern to follow.
close_reason: Design documents completed for both task-sync and github-task-sync skills. Documents cover SKILL.md structure, reference files, scripts, metadata, and integration patterns following skill-creator principles.

title: Monitoring Analysis Skill
description:
## Description
Create a new agentic skill for analyzing monitoring data (logs, metrics, spans, events, RUM) and triaging issues into beads bugs/tasks.

## Philosophy
This skill should be **highly agentic** - it provides workflow guidance but lets the LLM decide how to interpret data and what actions to take. No rigid parsing, thresholds, or coded rules.

## Key Concepts
- **MONITORING.md** - Project-specific config file (like RELEASING.md for github-releases)
  - Written by project owners
  - Describes how to query monitoring data
  - Describes what to look for / analyze
- **Two workflows**:
  1. Setup/Edit MONITORING.md - Help users create and maintain their config
  2. Analyze & Triage - Query data, identify issues, create/update beads

## The Agentic Approach
- Step 1 queries monitoring system, outputs something like issue-summary.md (just text)
- Step 2 reads that output, identifies issues, checks beads for duplicates
- Agent decides: create new bug? update existing? add comment? do nothing?
- No hard-coded thresholds or rules - LLM uses judgment

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Skill created in ai-resources/skills/monitoring-analysis/
- [ ] Two workflows documented (setup + analyze)
- [ ] Tested on opencode-coder project with real MONITORING.md
- [ ] Validated with skill-validator
close_reason: Epic complete. Skill created in ai-resources/skills/monitoring-analysis/ with two workflows (setup + analyze), tested on opencode-coder project (docs/MONITORING.md created), and validated with skill-validator. Evaluation notes captured with improvement suggestions for future refinement.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Skill Evaluation from oc-5mtm Testing

### What Worked Well
1. **Setup workflow was clear** - The setup-monitoring-config.md reference provided good guidance on structure for MONITORING.md
2. **Analyze workflow was straightforward** - The analyze-and-triage.md provided clear steps to follow
3. **Natural language approach worked** - The MONITORING.md I created captured domain knowledge well without being overly structured

### Issues Encountered
1. **Log location mismatch** - The MONITORING.md I created mentioned the wrong log path initially (~/.local/state/opencode/log/ vs actual ~/.local/share/opencode/log/). This is a project-specific config issue, not a skill issue.

2. **Session-based filtering was limiting** - The log-analyzer only showed 1 session despite many log files existing. Had to fall back to grep to find errors across all logs. The skill should perhaps mention that direct log file searching may be needed when session discovery is incomplete.

3. **Minor: issue-summary.md as working file** - The workflow suggests creating this file but doesn't specify where. Used project root which is fine but could be clearer.

### Results
- Created docs/MONITORING.md for opencode-coder
- Found 1 actual issue: ERROR log for missing knowledge-base/command directory
- Assessed as low priority (doesn't affect functionality)
- Did not create bug (below threshold for tracking)

### Improvement Suggestions
1. **Add fallback search guidance** - The analyze workflow could mention that direct log file searching (grep) may be needed when structured tools don't find everything

2. **Working file location** - Clarify where issue-summary.md should be created (temp dir vs project root vs don't create)

3. **Threshold guidance** - While the skill correctly avoids hard thresholds, some guidance on when NOT to create bugs would be helpful (e.g., 'errors that don't affect functionality and appear only in debug logs')

Overall: **Skill works well for its intended purpose**. The agentic approach with LLM judgment is correct - the issues I found were correctly assessed as low priority without creating noise bugs.

title: Coder Tool & Session Export
description:
## Description
Add a generic `coder` custom tool and a `/dump-session` command to the opencode-coder plugin.

The `coder` tool is an AI-callable tool that provides utility sub-commands:
- `session` — returns current session metadata
- `version` — returns coder plugin version
- `session-export <path>` — exports full session data to a folder (creates it if missing)
- `tokens` — returns token usage and context percentage

The `/dump-session` command is a user-invokable TUI command that exports the current session to `private/session-dump/<session-id>/` in the project directory.

## Goals
- Agents can programmatically access session data and export it
- Users can quickly dump a session for archival/review via `/dump-session`
- Full export includes: messages, tool calls, token usage, costs, metadata, file diffs
- Export format: structured JSON

## Technical Approach
1. Create `SessionExportService` — core logic for fetching and serializing session data
2. Register `coder` tool via the plugin `tool` hook (currently unused, available)
3. Register `/dump-session` command via knowledge-base command markdown
4. Wire the tool hook into `src/index.ts`

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `coder` tool registered and callable by AI agents
- [ ] All 4 sub-commands work: session, version, session-export, tokens
- [ ] `/dump-session` command exports full session to project directory
- [ ] Export creates folder structure if it doesn't exist
- [ ] JSON output contains all session data (messages, tools, tokens, diffs, metadata)
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: All tasks and gate complete. Coder tool (session, version, session-export, tokens) and /coder/dump-session command implemented successfully.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Review Report: oc-8ote

**Issue**: Coder Tool & Session Export
**Type**: epic
**Risk**: medium
**Status**: NEEDS_WORK

### Open Questions Check
- [x] No `has:open-questions` label present
- [x] All questions resolved

### Structure Assessment
- [x] Breakdown is logical — service → tool → wiring → command → gate
- [x] Dependencies are correct and properly ordered
- [x] Scope is appropriate — 4 implementation tasks + 1 gate

### Completeness Assessment
- [x] All requirements addressed
- [x] Edge cases considered (error handling for diff failures noted in oc-d5bf)
- [x] Error handling planned (tool returns error strings, service catches exceptions)

### Clarity Assessment
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are testable — mostly yes, but token field names are wrong (see bug oc-9h86)
- [ ] Instructions are complete — command task oc-ll2s has contradictory instructions about file path (see bug oc-4lcz)
- [x] Context is sufficient

### Risk Assessment
**Risk Level**: medium
**Rationale**: Two SDK API mismatches found. Token cache fields use nested structure (tokens.cache.read) not flat (tokens.cache_read). Command loader requires subdirectory structure that contradicts oc-ll2s instructions. Both are straightforward fixes but would cause silent failures if implemented as-is.

### Issues Found

1. **Issue**: Token cache field paths wrong in oc-d5bf
   - **Severity**: high
   - **Action**: Created bug oc-9h86. SDK uses tokens.cache.read/tokens.cache.write (nested), not tokens.cache_read/tokens.cache_write (flat). Would produce incorrect/NaN token summaries.

2. **Issue**: Command file placement wrong in oc-ll2s
   - **Severity**: high
   - **Action**: Created bug oc-4lcz. The command loader only reads .md files inside subdirectories. A flat knowledge-base/command/dump-session.md will be silently ignored. Must use knowledge-base/command/coder/dump-session.md (command becomes /coder/dump-session).

### Verified Correct

- client.session.get(), client.session.messages(), client.session.diff() — all confirmed with correct call signatures and return types
- tool hook on Hooks interface — confirmed as { [key: string]: ToolDefinition }
- tool() helper — confirmed: uses Zod schema, execute returns Promise<string>
- ToolContext — confirmed all referenced fields exist
- AssistantMessage.cost — confirmed as number
- Service constructor pattern — matches existing BeadsService
- getVersionInfo() — exists in src/core/version.ts, returns VersionInfo with name + version
- session.diff() returns Array<FileDiff> — confirmed, but can throw (404/400), so error handling is appropriate

### Actions Taken

| Action | Type | ID | Description |
|--------|------|-----|-------------|
| Created | bug | oc-9h86 | Token cache fields are nested (cache.read/write), not flat (cache_read/cache_write) |
| Created | bug | oc-4lcz | Command loader requires subdirectory — flat dump-session.md will be ignored |
| Comment | - | - | This review summary on epic oc-8ote |

### Recommendation

**NEEDS_WORK** — Two issues must be addressed before implementation:
1. oc-9h86: Implementer of oc-d5bf must use tokens.cache.read / tokens.cache.write instead of flat field names
2. oc-4lcz: Task oc-ll2s must place command file at knowledge-base/command/coder/dump-session.md and accept /coder/dump-session as the command name

Both are straightforward fixes. The rest of the plan is technically sound — all SDK APIs verified, architecture fits existing patterns, dependency chain is correct.

title: Incorrect dependency: oc-oxen should not depend on oc-dpee
description:
## Description
Task oc-oxen (Remove .coder/ folder from this project) currently depends on oc-dpee (Update all tests), but this dependency is incorrect. The .coder/ folder can be removed as soon as the config code is deleted (after oc-9tbu), independently of test updates.

## Current Situation
```
oc-9tbu (Remove config code)
  ↓
oc-dpee (Update tests) 
  ↓
oc-oxen (Remove .coder/ folder)  ← WRONG
```

## Correct Dependency
```
oc-9tbu (Remove config code)
  ↓
oc-oxen (Remove .coder/ folder)  ← Can happen immediately after config removal

oc-9tbu (Remove config code)
  ↓
oc-dpee (Update tests)  ← Can happen in parallel with oc-oxen
```

## Resolution Needed
1. Remove the dependency from oc-oxen to oc-dpee
2. Add dependency from oc-oxen to oc-9tbu (if not already present)
3. Keep oc-dpee depending on oc-9tbu (this is correct)

## Impact
- oc-oxen is unnecessarily blocked
- .coder/ folder removal could happen earlier in the workflow
- Better parallelization of test updates and folder cleanup

## Related To
Epic oc-caad - Remove .coder/ folder and simplify configuration
close_reason: Fixed: Removed oc-oxen → oc-dpee dependency, added oc-oxen → oc-9tbu dependency. Now oc-oxen and oc-dpee can run in parallel after oc-9tbu.

title: Add environment variable support for plugin control
description:
## Description
Add support for environment variables to control plugin behavior, replacing the config file options.

## Environment Variables to Add
1. `OPENCODE_CODER_DISABLED` - When set to "true", disable the entire plugin
2. `BEADS_AUTO_APPROVE` - When set to "false", require approval for bd commands (default: true)

## Instructions
1. Create new file `src/config/env.ts` with helper functions:
   - `isPluginDisabled(): boolean` - checks OPENCODE_CODER_DISABLED
   - `shouldAutoApproveBeads(): boolean` - checks BEADS_AUTO_APPROVE (default true)
2. Add type-safe env var parsing (handle "true"/"false" strings)
3. Add JSDoc comments explaining each env var
4. Export functions for use in services

## Files to Create
- src/config/env.ts - New env var helpers

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OPENCODE_CODER_DISABLED="true" returns true from isPluginDisabled()
- [ ] BEADS_AUTO_APPROVE="false" returns false from shouldAutoApproveBeads()
- [ ] Default values work when env vars not set
- [ ] Type-safe string-to-boolean conversion
- [ ] JSDoc comments document each env var
close_reason: Implemented env var helpers with type-safe parsing. Created isPluginDisabled() and shouldAutoApproveBeads() functions with comprehensive JSDoc documentation. Added 23 unit tests covering all edge cases. All 149 tests passing.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Review finding: Consider whether OPENCODE_CODER_DISABLED (negative naming) is the right choice vs OPENCODE_CODER_ENABLED=false (positive naming). Current choice is fine if 'kill switch' semantics are important, but worth discussing for consistency with other env vars like BEADS_AUTO_APPROVE.

description:
## Description
Major codebase restructuring to:
1. Remove the legacy `knowledge-base/` folder and ALL related code (loaders, service, paths, tests)
2. Rename the 4 beads agents to shorter names: orchestrator, reviewer, verifier, tasker
3. Create a new `committer` agent for git commit + push workflows
4. Create an `opencode-beads` skill skeleton with workflow philosophy and templates
5. Update the `opencode-coder` package to include all beads resources

## Context
The 4 beads agents have already been moved to `ai-resources/agents/`. The `knowledge-base/` folder is now empty (just an empty `agent/` subfolder). The entire `src/kb/` subsystem, `KnowledgeBaseService`, and `resolveKnowledgeBaseDir` are dead code. Users will install agents/commands via `aimgr` going forward.

## Goals
- Clean dead code: remove ~500 lines of unused knowledge-base loading infrastructure
- Simpler agent names: orchestrator, reviewer, verifier, tasker, committer
- New commit agent for the commit+push workflow (task agent will reference it)
- Skeleton for the opencode-beads skill (detailed content to be designed later)
- All beads resources registered in the opencode-coder package

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `knowledge-base/` folder deleted
- [ ] All src/kb/ code removed, src/core/paths.ts removed
- [ ] KnowledgeBaseService removed from src/service/ and src/index.ts
- [ ] All related tests cleaned up

- [ ] opencode-beads skill skeleton created

- [ ] Build passes (bun run build)
- [ ] Tests pass (bun test)
- [ ] Typecheck passes (bun run typecheck)

title: Create committer agent
description:
## Description
Create a new "committer" agent that handles git commit + push workflows. This agent will be called by the tasker agent when implementation is complete, providing a clean separation between coding and version control.

## Instructions

### 1. Create ai-resources/agents/committer.md

The agent should:
- Be a subagent (mode: subagent)
- Use a cost-efficient model (claude-sonnet)

Content should cover:
1. **Analyze changes**: Run `git status`, `git diff --staged`, `git diff` to understand what changed
2. **Stage files**: Stage relevant files, NEVER stage secrets (.env, credentials, etc.)
3. **Review recent commits**: Read recent `git log` to match the project's commit message style
4. **Write commit message**: Focus on "why" not "what", 1-2 sentences, conventional style
5. **Commit**: Create the commit
6. **Pull and push**: Run `git pull --rebase` then `git push`
7. **Verify**: Run `git status` to confirm branch is up to date with remote
8. **Report**: Summarize what was committed and pushed

Safety rules to include:
- NEVER force push
- NEVER amend commits that have been pushed
- NEVER skip hooks (no --no-verify)
- NEVER commit files that contain secrets
- If push fails, report the error — do not retry with force
- If pre-commit hook modifies files, stage the changes and amend (only if commit was just created and not pushed)

### 2. Update tasker agent to reference committer
Add a note to `ai-resources/agents/tasker.md` (the renamed beads-task-agent) in its workflow section:
- After completing implementation and verifying work, invoke the committer agent to commit and push changes
- Example: "When implementation is complete and verified, use the committer agent to commit and push your changes"

## Files to Create
- `ai-resources/agents/committer.md` (create)

## Files to Modify
- `ai-resources/agents/tasker.md` (add reference to committer agent — do this AFTER the rename task is done)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ai-resources/agents/committer.md` exists
- [ ] Has frontmatter: mode=subagent, description, model
- [ ] Covers: status check, staging, commit message writing, commit, pull --rebase, push, verification
- [ ] Includes safety rules (no force push, no secrets, no skip hooks)
- [ ] tasker.md references the committer agent in its workflow

## Notes
This depends on the rename task (oc-8vtb.3) being done first, since it needs to update tasker.md (the renamed file).
close_reason: Created ai-resources/agents/committer.md with full commit/push workflow and safety rules. Updated tasker.md with step 6 (Commit and Push) referencing the committer agent. Updated orchestrator.md agent table to include committer.

title: Source code still references old agent names after rename
description:
## Description

During verification of gate oc-8vtb.1, found that 20 references to old agent names remain in the source code after agents were renamed from `beads-*-agent` to shorter names.

## Found During

Verification of gate oc-8vtb.1 (Acceptance Gate: Codebase restructuring)

## Details

The agents in `ai-resources/agents/` were correctly renamed:
- `beads-planner-agent` → `orchestrator`
- `beads-review-agent` → `reviewer`  
- `beads-task-agent` → `tasker`
- `beads-verify-agent` → `verifier`

But the source code still references the OLD names:

### `src/service/beads-service.ts` (12 references)
- Line 112: `config.default_agent = "beads-planner-agent"` — should be `"orchestrator"`
- Lines 118-136: Permission configuration targets `beads-planner-agent` — should target `orchestrator`

### `src/beads/context.ts` (8 references)
- Lines 58-61: Agent table lists old names
- Lines 74-87: Delegation guidance references old names (`beads-planner-agent`, `beads-review-agent`, `beads-task-agent`, `beads-verify-agent`)

### `tests/` (6 references)
- `tests/unit/beads-service.test.ts`: Asserts old name `beads-planner-agent`
- `tests/unit/context.test.ts`: Asserts old name `beads-task-agent`

## Impact

The plugin will:
1. Set `default_agent` to a non-existent agent name
2. Configure permissions for a non-existent agent
3. Guide users to delegate to non-existent agents

## Expected Behavior

All source code references should use the new agent names: `orchestrator`, `reviewer`, `tasker`, `verifier`, `committer`.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `grep -r 'beads-.*-agent' src/`
2. Observe 20 matches referencing old agent names
3. Compare with `ls ai-resources/agents/` which shows new names
close_reason: All old agent name references updated to new names in src/ and tests/. Changed beads-planner-agent→orchestrator, beads-review-agent→reviewer, beads-task-agent→tasker, beads-verify-agent→verifier across 4 files. Verified: grep returns no matches, typecheck passes, all 99 tests pass.

title: Replace post-commit references in all documentation files (GitHub #5)
description:
## Description

Remove all references to non-existent post-commit hook and replace with correct hook references (pre-commit). Beads installs 5 hooks, not 6.

## Background

The post-commit hook NEVER existed in beads. Beads installs these 5 hooks:
1. pre-commit - Flush changes to JSONL before commit
2. post-merge - Import JSONL after pull/merge
3. pre-push - Prevent pushing stale JSONL
4. post-checkout - Import JSONL after branch checkout
5. prepare-commit-msg - Add agent identity trailers

## Instructions

### 1. Fix `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/status.md`

Search for `post-commit` references and replace with `pre-commit`

**Find**: `test -f .git/hooks/post-commit`
**Replace with**: `test -f .git/hooks/pre-commit`

### 2. Fix `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/SKILL.md`

Search for `post-commit` references and update to reference correct hooks.

Update any list of hooks from "6 hooks" to "5 hooks":
- prepare-commit-msg ✓
- pre-commit ✓
- post-merge ✓
- pre-push ✓
- post-checkout ✓
- ~~post-commit~~ ❌ (remove)

### 3. Fix `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md`

Around lines 157-159, replace:
```bash
if [ -f ".git/hooks/post-commit" ]; then
    echo "post-commit: INSTALLED"
```

With:
```bash
if [ -f ".git/hooks/pre-commit" ]; then
    echo "pre-commit: INSTALLED"
```

Update all other `post-commit` references similarly.

### 4. Fix `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/troubleshooting-patterns.md`

Around lines 136-137, 208, 213:
- Replace `post-commit` with `pre-commit`
- Update hook count from 6 to 5 if mentioned
- Ensure troubleshooting steps reference correct hooks

### 5. Verify Changes

After all replacements, search entire codebase for remaining `post-commit` references:
```bash
grep -r "post-commit" ai-resources/ .opencode/
```

Should only find references in git-related contexts, not beads hook documentation.

## Files to Modify

- `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/status.md` - Replace post-commit with pre-commit
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/SKILL.md` - Update hook list and counts
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md` - Fix status checks
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/troubleshooting-patterns.md` - Fix troubleshooting steps

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All `post-commit` references replaced with `pre-commit` in affected files
- [ ] Hook count updated from 6 to 5 where mentioned
- [ ] Status check scripts validate correct 5 hooks
- [ ] No remaining incorrect post-commit references in documentation
- [ ] Grep verification shows no post-commit in beads hook docs
- [ ] File saved and changes verified

## Related Issues

- Fixes GitHub issue #5
- Related to bead: oc-stdz
close_reason:
Replaced all post-commit references with pre-commit in documentation files. The non-existent post-commit hook references have been corrected. Beads installs 5 hooks (pre-commit, post-merge, pre-push, post-checkout, prepare-commit-msg), not 6. All affected files have been updated:
- ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/status.md
- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/SKILL.md  
- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md
- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/troubleshooting-patterns.md

Verification completed - no remaining incorrect post-commit references found in documentation.

title: Investigate why agent permission config in BeadsService doesn't enable question tool
description:
## Description
The programmatic agent permission configuration added to BeadsService.processConfig() is not enabling the question tool for beads-planner-agent, even after OpenCode restart.

## What We've Tried
1. ✅ Added frontmatter permissions to markdown file - didn't work
2. ✅ Added programmatic config in BeadsService.processConfig():
   ```typescript
   config.agent["beads-planner-agent"].permission.question = "allow";
   config.agent["beads-planner-agent"].permission.edit = { "*": "deny" };
   ```
3. ✅ Restarted OpenCode
4. ✅ Added debug logging
5. ❌ Question tool still not available after restart

## Current Status
- User restarted OpenCode
- beads-planner-agent is active (default agent)
- Question tool still shows as unavailable
- Agent still cannot access question tool

## Root Cause Hypothesis
**Plugin agents from `knowledge-base/agent/` directories may not support permission configuration the same way as native or config-based agents.**

Possible issues:
1. Plugin agents may be loaded AFTER config hook runs
2. Plugin agent markdown frontmatter may override programmatic config
3. There may be no way to set permissions for plugin-defined agents
4. Plugin agents may need to be defined in opencode.json instead of knowledge-base/

## Next Investigation Steps
1. Check if we need to define beads-planner-agent in opencode.json instead
2. Test if config-based agent definition works with question tool
3. Check OpenCode documentation for plugin agent limitations
4. Look at how other plugins define agents with custom permissions
5. Consider if we need to contribute to OpenCode to support this use case

## Alternative Approaches to Try
### Approach 1: Define agent in opencode.json
Instead of markdown in knowledge-base/, define in config:
```json
{
  "agent": {
    "beads-planner-agent": {
      "mode": "primary",
      "prompt": "{file:knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md}",
      "permission": {
        "question": "allow",
        "edit": {
          "*": "deny"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Approach 2: Modify default agent
Set default_agent to "plan" instead, and inject beads context into plan agent.

### Approach 3: Use config hook differently
Try merging agent config instead of setting directly.

## Files to Check
- OpenCode docs: Plugin agent capabilities
- OpenCode source: How plugin agents are loaded
- Other plugins: How they define custom agents
close_reason: Root cause found: AgentDef type and loading system didn't support permission field. Fixed by adding permission support to type, loader, and registration. Implemented in oc-y9xb.
