title: Add project context discovery to generic agents
description:
## Description
The agents in `ai-resources/agents/` (tasker, verifier, reviewer, orchestrator) are generic — they ship with the plugin and run in any project. Currently they are blind to project-specific conventions: the tasker doesn't know the coding style, the verifier hardcodes `bun test`, the reviewer judges against generic best practices instead of project standards.

Since OpenCode automatically injects AGENTS.md into all agent contexts, agents already HAVE project-specific info — they just don't know to use it or follow its references to deeper docs (CONTRIBUTING.md, coding guidelines, etc.).

## Goals
- Each agent acknowledges and uses project-specific instructions from its injected context
- Each agent follows references to deeper docs (CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/coding-guidelines.md, etc.) when relevant
- No hardcoded project-specific commands (remove `bun test`, `bun run build`, etc.)
- Aligned with what `/init` creates in AGENTS.md (via agents-md-template.md)

## Scope
Files to modify:
- `ai-resources/agents/tasker.md`
- `ai-resources/agents/verifier.md`
- `ai-resources/agents/reviewer.md`
- `ai-resources/agents/orchestrator.md`

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 4 agents updated
- [ ] No hardcoded project-specific commands remain
- [ ] Agents reference project context without assuming specific filenames beyond AGENTS.md
- [ ] Aligned with agents-md-template.md sections
close_reason: All tasks completed, gate verified. All 4 agents updated with Project Context sections, no hardcoded commands, skill validated.

title: Template variables {{name}}, {{description}}, {{mode}} not found during KB rendering
description:
## Problem

During plugin loading, multiple warnings are logged about missing template variables:

```
WARN [opencode-coder] Template variable not found: {{name}}
WARN [opencode-coder] Template variable not found: {{description}}
WARN [opencode-coder] Template variable not found: {{mode}}
```

These appear 18 times during the loading of 8 commands and 1 agent.

## Root Cause

Knowledge-base templates (commands/agents) contain Mustache variables like `{{name}}`, `{{description}}`, and `{{mode}}` that are not being provided by the TemplateService during rendering.

## Expected Behavior

Either:
1. These variables should be provided by the template context
2. Or the templates should not reference variables that aren't available

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start OpenCode with the plugin enabled
2. Check logs: `bun run logs --service=opencode-coder --level=WARN`

## Files to Investigate

- `src/template/template-service.ts` - Template rendering logic
- `knowledge-base/` - Templates that may reference these variables
close_reason: Fixed by skipping validation for all section-scoped variables, not just array sections. Renamed insideArraySection to insideSection for clarity.

title: Add parallel execution emphasis to Phase 1 and Phase 5
description:
## Description
Enhance Phase 1 (Discovery) and Phase 5 (Execution) with explicit parallel execution guidance.

## Instructions

### Phase 1 Enhancement (around line 211-218)
Update research step to:
```markdown
2. **Research codebase** (grep, glob, read files)
   - **For complex/uncertain scope:** Launch multiple explore agents IN PARALLEL
     - Use single message with multiple task tool calls
     - Each agent focuses on different aspect:
       * Agent 1: Existing similar implementations
       * Agent 2: Current architecture patterns  
       * Agent 3: Testing approaches
     - Maximum 3 agents (quality over quantity)
   - **For simple/known tasks:** Direct grep/read is sufficient
```

### Phase 5 Enhancement (around line 248-256)
Replace existing execution section with:
```markdown
### Phase 5: Execution
When user approves:

**For single tasks:**
Task(subagent_type: "beads-task-agent", prompt: "Execute task beads-xxx...")

**For multiple independent tasks (PREFERRED when possible):**
Launch multiple task agents IN PARALLEL using a single message with multiple tool calls.

**Continue until blocked:**
- Check `bd ready` for newly unblocked work
- Spawn agents for ready tasks
- When no ready tasks remain, proceed to verification
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Phase 1 has parallel explore guidance
- [ ] Phase 5 emphasizes parallel task execution
- [ ] Clear examples of when to use parallel vs sequential
- [ ] "Single message with multiple tool calls" pattern explained
- [ ] Maximum 3 agents guidance included

## Files to Modify
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md (lines ~211-218, ~248-256)
close_reason: Added parallel execution emphasis to Phase 1 (explore agents for complex research) and Phase 5 (task agents for independent work). Includes: max 3 agents guideline, single message with multiple tool calls pattern, and clear when-to-use guidance for parallel vs sequential.

description:

title: Epic Acceptance: GitHub Issues Sync
description:
## Gate Criteria

- [ ] All sync tasks closed
- [ ] Command can import GitHub issues to beads
- [ ] Command can close GitHub issues when beads issues are closed
- [ ] User interaction for working on open bugs works
- [ ] Integration tested with real GitHub issues
- [ ] No regressions to existing /coder/feedback command

## Owner

beads-verify-agent

## Verification Steps

1. Run `/coder/sync-github-issues` on a repo with open GitHub issues
2. Verify beads issues are created with correct metadata and `source:external` label
3. Close a synced beads issue
4. Run sync again and verify GitHub issue is closed with proper message
5. Verify open bug prompting works correctly

title: Convert phases to task templates
description:
## Description

Convert each release phase into a copy-pasteable task template that agents can use directly when creating their release tasks.

## Instructions

1. Open `ai-resources/skills/github-releases/references/release-workflow.md`
2. For EACH phase (Quality Gates, Documentation, Version Bump, Create Release, Release Notes), add a task template subsection
3. Use this format for each phase:

### Example for Phase 1: Quality Gates

```markdown
## Phase 1: Quality Gates

### Task Template

Create this task for your release:

```markdown
### Task: Quality Gates for vX.Y.Z

## Instructions
1. Verify clean working tree: `git status`
2. Run full test suite: [use command from RELEASING.md]
3. Run build: [use command from RELEASING.md]
4. Check CI status: `gh run list --limit 1`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Working tree is clean (zero uncommitted changes)
- [ ] ALL tests pass (zero failures - no exceptions)
- [ ] Build succeeds with no errors
- [ ] CI is green on main branch

## STOP Conditions
If ANY criterion fails:
1. STOP immediately
2. Do NOT proceed to next phase
3. Report failure to user
```

Spawn beads-task-agent to execute this task.
```

4. Repeat this pattern for all 5 phases:
   - Phase 1: Quality Gates
   - Phase 2: Documentation
   - Phase 3: Version Bump
   - Phase 4: Create Release
   - Phase 5: Release Notes

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 phases have task templates
- [ ] Each template has Instructions, Acceptance Criteria, STOP Conditions
- [ ] Templates are in markdown code blocks for easy copying
- [ ] Each template ends with "Spawn beads-task-agent to execute"

## Files to Modify

- ai-resources/skills/github-releases/references/release-workflow.md (update)
close_reason: Added task templates to all 5 phases (Quality Gates, Documentation, Version Bump, Create Release, Release Notes). Each template includes Instructions, Acceptance Criteria, STOP Conditions in markdown code blocks, ending with 'Spawn beads-task-agent to execute'.

title: Implement beads-to-GitHub sync (close GitHub issues)
description:
## Description

When a beads issue that was imported from GitHub is closed, sync that status back to GitHub by closing the corresponding GitHub issue with an appropriate message.

## Instructions

1. Find beads issues that:
   - Have `source:external` label
   - Are closed
   - Have a GitHub issue reference (URL or `#N` in description/notes)
2. For each such beads issue:
   - Check if the GitHub issue is still open
   - If open, close it with a comment summarizing the resolution
3. Generate the close message from:
   - The beads issue close reason (if provided)
   - Recent comments on the beads issue
   - Or a default "Resolved in internal tracking" message

## GitHub CLI Commands

```bash
# Close issue with comment
gh issue close <number> --comment "Resolved. <summary from beads>"

# Or add comment first, then close
gh issue comment <number> --body "Resolution: ..."
gh issue close <number>
```

## Message Format

```
## Resolution

This issue has been resolved.

**Summary**: <from beads close reason or comments>

---
*Synced from internal issue tracking*
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detects closed beads issues with GitHub references
- [ ] Closes corresponding GitHub issues
- [ ] Includes meaningful resolution message from beads
- [ ] Handles case where GitHub issue is already closed
- [ ] Reports sync results to user
close_reason: Beads-to-GitHub sync already implemented in sync-github-issues.md Step 4 with all acceptance criteria covered

title: GitHub commands shown even when GitHub not available
description:
## Description

The gate criterion "Command not shown when GitHub not available" is NOT implemented in the current codebase.

Currently, commands in the `github/` folder (like `/github/sync-issues`) are always registered regardless of whether GitHub integration is enabled.

## Found During

Verification of gate oc-i8xa (Epic Acceptance: GitHub Integration)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set up an environment without GitHub (no `.git` folder, or no GitHub remote, or no `gh` CLI)
2. Load the OpenCode plugin
3. Run `/coder/status` or check available commands
4. Observe that `/github/sync-issues` is still shown

## Expected Behavior

- Commands in the `github/` folder should NOT be registered when `github.enabled` is false
- Or, commands should have a way to declare their feature requirements

## Actual Behavior

- All commands are registered regardless of integration status
- `/github/sync-issues` appears even when GitHub is not available

## Technical Details

The `KnowledgeBaseService.processConfig()` method loads ALL commands from all knowledge bases without any filtering based on `github.enabled` (or any other feature flag).

## Suggested Approaches

1. **Folder-based filtering**: Filter commands based on their folder (e.g., `github/*` only when `github.enabled`)
2. **Frontmatter requires**: Add a `requires: [github]` field to command frontmatter
3. **Convention-based**: Commands in feature-specific folders auto-require that feature

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Commands in `github/` folder only shown when `github.enabled` is true
- [ ] When GitHub is not available, `/github/sync-issues` should not appear in command list
- [ ] `/coder/status` correctly reflects which commands are available
close_reason: Implemented convention-based command filtering: commands in 'github/' folder are only registered when github.enabled is true. Added FeatureFlags to KnowledgeBaseService, shouldRegisterCommand() method for filtering, and updated template service to receive filtered commands so /coder/status shows accurate command list. All unit and integration tests pass.

title: Simplify plugin status reporting and remove unnecessary system_info complexity
description:
## Description

The plugin status command currently has over-engineered complexity:
1. Plugin code tries to dynamically find and read package.json at runtime (fails when bundled)
2. system_info tool returns "unknown" for plugin version due to path resolution issues
3. Status command relies on system_info when it could just read package.json directly

**Philosophy**: "Best code is no code" - Let the LLM read package.json directly instead of bundling complex runtime logic into the plugin.

## Goals

1. **Simplify**: Remove unnecessary complexity from plugin code
2. **Improve**: Make status command work reliably without plugin version detection
3. **Document**: Clear skill instructions for checking plugin status
4. **Clean**: Remove unused code paths and update tests/docs

## Scope

### Analysis Phase
- Audit all usage of system_info tool (in code and docs)
- Determine what information is truly needed vs nice-to-have
- Identify what can be replaced with simple bash/file reads

### Implementation Phase
- Simplify or remove plugin version detection code
- Update status command to read package.json directly
- Create dedicated skill instructions for status checking
- Update all documentation references

### Verification Phase
- Test status command works in both dev and installed scenarios
- Verify no breaking changes to system_info consumers
- Update or remove related tests

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Status command reliably shows correct plugin version
- [ ] Plugin code simplified (less runtime complexity)
- [ ] Skill has clear, tested instructions for status checks
- [ ] All documentation updated and accurate
- [ ] Tests pass (or updated/removed if obsolete)

## Context

Current system_info provides:
- **SessionInfo**: sessionID, pid, workingDir, command, terminal
- **PluginInfo**: name, version (broken), active status
- **BeadsInfo**: enabled, cliVersion, projectInitialized, stats

Question: What of this is truly needed vs can be obtained via simple bash commands?

## Related Files

- `src/system-info/` - Tool implementation
- `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/status.md` - Status command
- `.opencode/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md` - Skill reference
- `src/index.ts` - Tool registration
close_reason: Epic complete! Removed system_info tool (212 lines) in favor of bash commands. All tasks completed: analysis, skill docs, implementation, testing, documentation updates. Status command now works reliably. Philosophy validated: best code is no code.

title: [agent] Task agents don't use session sandbox folder (github:#4)
description:
## Original GitHub Issue

**GitHub**: hk9890/opencode-coder#4
**Created**: 2026-01-20T17:42:15Z

## Description

## Problem
Task agents (beads-task-agent) spawned via the Task tool do not use the provided session sandbox folder. Instead, they attempt to write to arbitrary `/tmp/` paths, ignoring the session-specific sandbox directory.

## Expected Behavior
When a task agent is spawned in a session with a sandbox folder (e.g., `/tmp/opencode/ses_423be8360ffeCKYKn5y8EiHzVD`), the agent should:
- Be aware of the sandbox folder path
- Use it for all temporary file operations
- Respect session isolation

## Actual Behavior
Task agents ignore the sandbox folder and try to write directly to `/tmp/something` or other arbitrary paths, breaking session isolation.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a session with sandbox folder active
2. Planner agent correctly identifies sandbox path: `/tmp/opencode/ses_423be8360ffeCKYKn5y8EiHzVD`
3. Spawn a task agent via Task tool
4. Task agent attempts file operations in `/tmp/` instead of sandbox

## Impact
- Violates session isolation principle
- Potential file conflicts between concurrent sessions
- Makes cleanup unreliable
- Breaks the documented sandbox workflow
- Subagents appear unaware of sandbox context

## Environment
- Session context: Testing sandbox folder functionality with beads workflow
- Commands involved: Task tool spawning beads-task-agent
- Session sandbox: `/tmp/opencode/ses_423be8360ffeCKYKn5y8EiHzVD`

## Additional Context
The planner agent (beads-planner-agent) correctly receives and can reference the sandbox folder path, but when delegating work to task agents, this context is not properly propagated or the task agents don't respect it.

## Suggested Fix
Task agents should either:
1. Receive the sandbox folder path as part of their initialization context
2. Have access to a session environment variable pointing to the sandbox
3. Default to using the sandbox for any temporary file operations

---
*Imported from GitHub via /github/sync-issues*
close_reason: Solution already implemented: playground context updated (line 151 in context.ts) and permission hook auto-approves all temp directory operations (lines 285-295 in beads-service.ts)

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Root Cause Analysis: Task Agents Don't Use Session Sandbox

### Problem Confirmed
Task agents spawned via the Task tool do NOT receive the session sandbox (playground) context, despite the planner agent receiving it correctly.

### Why This Happens

**Context Injection Flow:**
1. `BeadsService.processChatMessage()` (line 196-251) hooks into `chat.message` event
2. Creates playground: `playgroundService.getOrCreatePlayground(sessionID)` (line 203)
3. Injects context with playground path via synthetic message (line 247)
4. Context is injected ONCE per session, tracked in `injectedSessions` Set (line 209)

**The Gap:**
- The `chat.message` hook ONLY fires for **user messages**
- When Task tool spawns a subagent, OpenCode creates a **new agent context**
- This new context does NOT include the playground synthetic message
- The plugin has NO hook to intercept subagent creation
- Result: Task agents start with a clean slate, missing playground context

### Evidence

**Planner receives playground (lines 142-161 in context.ts):**
```
## 🎮 Session Playground
**Path**: `/tmp/opencode/ses_423643fc1ffeavpgPHaTBUDJbV`
```

**Task agent frontmatter (lines 4-7 in beads-task-agent.md):**
```yaml
permission:
  write:
    "${TMPDIR}/opencode/**/*": allow
```

**Result:** Permissions work, but awareness doesn't. Task agents CAN write to playground if told, but they don't KNOW it exists.

### Architectural Constraint

The Task tool is **built into OpenCode core**, not this plugin. The plugin cannot control:
- How Task spawns subagents
- What context is passed to subagents
- When subagents are initialized

Available hooks in this plugin:
- ✅ `chat.message` - fires on user messages
- ✅ `session.compacted` - fires after compaction
- ❌ NO `subagent.create` or `agent.context` hook

### Code References

**Context Building:**
- `src/beads/context.ts:142-161` - Playground context generation
- `src/beads/context.ts:166-184` - Context string building

**Context Injection:**
- `src/service/beads-service.ts:196-251` - Chat message hook
- `src/service/beads-service.ts:310-360` - Injection via synthetic message

**Playground Creation:**
- `src/service/playground-service.ts:140-150` - Path creation

**Agent Configuration:**
- `knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md:4-30` - Planner permissions
- `knowledge-base/agent/beads-task-agent.md:4-7` - Task agent permissions

### Solution Options

**Option A: Include playground in Task prompts (Workaround)**
Modify planner to pass playground path explicitly:
```
Task(
  subagent_type: "beads-task-agent",
  prompt: "Execute task beads-xxx. Use playground: /tmp/opencode/ses_12345"
)
```
Pros: Works immediately, no SDK changes
Cons: Brittle, easy to forget, not scalable

**Option B: Request OpenCode SDK enhancement (Long-term)**
Add hook for subagent context injection:
- `subagent.create` hook or
- `agent.context` hook
Pros: Clean, scalable, proper solution
Cons: Requires platform changes, timeline unclear

**Option C: Session-level context (Needs investigation)**
Inject playground at session config level instead of synthetic message
Problem: No clear hook for "session started"

### Recommendation

**Immediate:** Implement Option A (workaround in planner prompt)
**Long-term:** Request Option B (SDK enhancement) from OpenCode team

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
# CORRECTED Analysis: Subagent Session Isolation

## What I Verified

### Test Results ✅

**Claim:** "Subagents don't receive context"  
**Verification:** Created test task oc-kkwb and spawned task agent  
**Result:** **FALSE** - Subagents DO receive full context including:
- ✅ Playground folder path
- ✅ Beads context (`bd prime` output)
- ✅ Agent architecture guidance

**Evidence:**
- Task agent created file: `/tmp/opencode/ses_42353e46bffene2aEhgrWXMN7j/context-test-report.txt`
- Task agent explicitly confirmed seeing all context elements
- Task agent properly used playground for temporary files

## The REAL Problem

### Session Isolation, Not Context Loss

**What actually happens:**

```
Parent Session:  ses_423643fc1ffeavpgPHaTBUDJbV
├── Playground: /tmp/opencode/ses_423643fc1ffeavpgPHaTBUDJbV/
├── Planner agent operates here
└── Spawns Task agent via Task tool
    ↓
Child Session:   ses_42353e46bffene2aEhgrWXMN7j  <-- NEW SESSION
├── Playground: /tmp/opencode/ses_42353e46bffene2aEhgrWXMN7j/  <-- NEW PLAYGROUND
└── Task agent operates here (ISOLATED)
```

**Problem:**
- Parent and child sessions have **different playgrounds**
- Files created by task agent are NOT accessible to planner
- Each subagent gets its own isolated workspace
- No shared workspace for the logical "session"

## Why This Matters

### Use Case 1: Planner wants task agent to process files

**Scenario:**
1. Planner writes data file to its playground: `ses_123/data.json`
2. Planner spawns task agent to process it
3. Task agent looks in ITS playground: `ses_456/data.json` ❌ NOT FOUND

### Use Case 2: Task agent creates output for planner

**Scenario:**
1. Task agent generates report: `ses_456/report.txt`
2. Task agent completes
3. Planner tries to read report: `ses_123/report.txt` ❌ NOT FOUND

### Use Case 3: Multiple task agents need shared state

**Scenario:**
1. Task agent A writes: `ses_456/state.db`
2. Task agent B needs to read it: `ses_789/state.db` ❌ NOT FOUND
3. Each agent has its own isolated playground

## What Was Wrong in My Analysis

### Incorrect Assumptions ❌

1. **"Subagents don't get context injected"**
   - Wrong: They DO get context injected via chat.message hook
   - Each new session triggers the injection

2. **"chat.message only fires for user messages"**  
   - Technically true, but subagents START their own sessions
   - Their first message triggers chat.message hook

3. **"Subagents operate with reduced context"**
   - Wrong: They get FULL context including playground path
   - Just a DIFFERENT playground than parent

### What I Got Right ✅

1. **Each subagent gets its own session ID**
   - Verified: Parent `ses_423...`, Child `ses_423...` (different)

2. **Playground service creates per-session folders**
   - Verified: Each session ID gets unique playground folder

3. **Context injection happens in processChatMessage**
   - Verified: Code analysis was correct for HOW it works
   - Just wrong about WHEN it fires

## The Architectural Question

**Is this a bug or by design?**

### Arguments for "Bug"
- Users expect shared workspace within a logical session
- Original issue says "Task agents ignore the sandbox folder"
- Parent can't share files with children
- Session isolation prevents collaboration

### Arguments for "By Design"  
- Subagents are independent sessions (by OpenCode design)
- Session isolation prevents accidental state pollution
- Each agent has clean workspace
- Parallel agents don't interfere with each other

## What the Original Issue Actually Says

> "When a task agent is spawned in a session with a sandbox folder (e.g., `/tmp/opencode/ses_423...`), the agent should:
> - Be aware of the sandbox folder path ✅ (but it's THEIR OWN path)
> - Use it for all temporary file operations ✅ (they do!)
> - Respect session isolation" ✅ (they do... TOO WELL!)

**The problem:** The issue author expected **parent's sandbox**, not **new sandbox**.

## Verification Method

I verified this by:

1. ✅ Created test task asking subagent to report what it sees
2. ✅ Spawned task agent via Task tool
3. ✅ Task agent reported seeing ALL context
4. ✅ Checked session IDs: Parent vs Child (DIFFERENT)
5. ✅ Checked playground folders: Both exist (SEPARATE)
6. ✅ Verified subagent wrote to ITS playground, not parent's

## Updated Solution Options

### Option A: Share Parent's Playground (If this is desired behavior)

Modify Task tool invocation to pass parent's playground path:
```
Task(
  subagent_type: "beads-task-agent",
  prompt: "Execute task. Use parent playground: /tmp/opencode/ses_123 for shared files"
)
```

But subagent would STILL see its own playground in system prompt.
This creates confusion: "Use ses_123" vs system says "Use ses_456"

### Option B: Mount/Link Parent Playground (Requires SDK enhancement)

OpenCode could support shared playgrounds:
- Parent session: `ses_123`
- Child inherits: Same `ses_123` OR mount point to parent

This requires OpenCode platform changes.

### Option C: Accept Isolation (Maybe it's correct?)

Perhaps this IS the correct behavior:
- Each agent truly independent
- No shared state needed
- Project codebase is the shared medium
- Playground is for truly temporary, agent-local files

## Next Steps

**Need to determine:**
1. Is session isolation intended, or is shared playground expected?
2. What's the use case for shared playground between parent/child?
3. Should we clarify documentation vs change behavior?

**Questions for user/issue author:**
- What specific files need to be shared between planner and task agents?
- Is the problem that task agents use `/tmp/` instead of ANY playground?
- Or is the problem that they use DIFFERENT playground than parent?

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Proposed solution: Auto-approve all /tmp/ writes (not just playground). This removes permission prompts while keeping playground feature available for agents that choose to use it. Simple 1-line fix in processPermissionAsk().

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
SOLUTION IMPLEMENTED:

1. Updated playground context instruction:
   - Changed from: 'DO NOT use /tmp or other temp locations directly'
   - Changed to: 'DO NOT use /tmp or other temp locations but use this <playground-path>'
   - More positive, includes the actual path in the instruction

2. Updated permission hook to auto-approve ALL temp directory operations:
   - Now approves any write/read in ${TMPDIR}/* (not just /tmp/opencode/*)
   - Cross-platform: handles TMPDIR, TEMP env vars, and /tmp fallback
   - No more permission prompts for temp files

Result: Agents can use /tmp/* freely without prompts, but are still encouraged to use the session-specific playground folder for better organization.

title: Documentation Audit: Fix inconsistencies across README, CONTRIBUTING, and AGENTS
description:
## Description
Comprehensive audit of the three main documentation files found multiple issues that need fixing to ensure accuracy and consistency with the actual codebase.

## Issues Found

### 1. **CRITICAL: Missing coder/* commands** (README.md)
**Issue**: README.md documents 5 coder commands that do NOT exist:
- `/coder/install` - Not found in knowledge-base or .opencode
- `/coder/init` - Not found
- `/coder/doctor` - Not found
- `/coder/status` - Not found
- `/coder/feedback` - Not found

**Evidence**: Searched entire codebase - no coder namespace commands exist
**Location**: README.md lines 110-119 (coder/* commands table)
**Fix Required**: Remove the entire coder/* commands section from README.md

### 2. **Incorrect Project Structure** (CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/coding-guidelines.md)
**Issue**: Documentation lists `src/template/` directory that does NOT exist
**Evidence**: `ls src/template/` returns "No such file or directory"
**Locations**:
- CONTRIBUTING.md line 38: `├── template/          # Template rendering with Mustache`
- docs/coding-guidelines.md line 20: `├── template/          # Template rendering with Mustache`

**Fix Required**: Remove `src/template/` from both project structure listings

### 3. **Missing src/system-info/** (CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/coding-guidelines.md)
**Issue**: Actual `src/system-info/` directory exists but not documented
**Evidence**: Directory exists with `tool.ts`, `types.ts`, and `collectors/` subdirectory
**Locations**: Both CONTRIBUTING.md and coding-guidelines.md project structure
**Fix Required**: Add `src/system-info/` to project structure in both files

### 4. **Inconsistent Test Script Names** (CONTRIBUTING.md)
**Issue**: CONTRIBUTING.md documents incorrect test script names
**Current in CONTRIBUTING.md line 126**: `bun test tests/unit`
**Actual in package.json**: `bun test tests/unit` (correct)
**BUT package.json also has**: `test:unit`, `test:integration`, `test:e2e`

**Fix Required**: Update CONTRIBUTING.md scripts table to show both forms:
- Direct: `bun test tests/unit`
- Named script: `bun run test:unit`

### 5. **README.md Quick Start Section References Non-existent Commands**
**Issue**: Lines 84-88 show example usage of coder commands that don't exist
**Fix Required**: Replace with actual beads workflow examples or remove section

## Verification Checklist

After fixes, verify:
- [ ] No coder/* command references remain in README.md
- [ ] src/template/ removed from project structure in CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] src/template/ removed from project structure in docs/coding-guidelines.md
- [ ] src/system-info/ added to project structure in both files
- [ ] Test script commands in CONTRIBUTING.md match package.json
- [ ] All file paths referenced in docs exist in actual codebase
- [ ] README.md Quick Start section uses only real commands

## Files to Modify
- README.md (remove coder/* commands section, fix Quick Start)
- CONTRIBUTING.md (fix project structure, verify test scripts)
- docs/coding-guidelines.md (fix project structure)

## Impact
**High Priority** - Documentation actively misleads users about available commands and project structure.

title: Design AGENTS.md template structure
description:
## Description

Design the standardized AGENTS.md template structure that will be generated by the /init command. This template should be adaptive based on installed skills and project structure.

## Instructions

1. Create a reference document in `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/agents-md-template.md`
2. Define the template structure with placeholders:
   - Project overview section (how to generate from codebase analysis)
   - Use case sections (which ones, how to detect if applicable)
   - Landing the plane section (conditional on beads)
   - Project-specific context (build commands, conventions)
3. Document the skill detection logic:
   - How to check if a skill is installed for a use case
   - How to dynamically include/exclude sections
   - Generic language patterns (don't hardcode skill names)
4. Define the total target size: ~150 lines
5. Include examples of good vs bad AGENTS.md structures

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Template structure documented in reference file
- [ ] Skill detection logic clearly defined
- [ ] Examples provided for different project types
- [ ] Template is generic and adaptive
- [ ] Document includes guidance on section sizing

## Files to Create

- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/agents-md-template.md`

## Notes

This is the foundation for the entire /init improvement. Get this right and implementation will be straightforward.
close_reason:
Created comprehensive AGENTS.md template reference document at ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/agents-md-template.md (460 lines).

Key design decisions:
1. Template Structure: Project Overview (always) + Development (always) + conditional sections based on skills/beads
2. Skill Detection: Two methods - check .opencode/skills/ directory (recommended) or aimgr list
3. Beads Detection: Check bd --version && ls .beads/
4. Edge Cases: Minimal project (~25-30 lines with just Project Overview + Development), handled explicitly
5. Existing AGENTS.md: Update in-place, preserving custom sections, removing outdated skill sections
6. Generic Language: Use 'load the release skill' not 'load github-releases skill'

Examples provided:
- Full TypeScript project with release skill (~120 lines)
- Minimal Python project with no skills (~30 lines)

Addressed review feedback: Added explicit 'Minimal Project (No Skills Installed)' section in Edge Cases.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: **Review Feedback**: Task looks well-structured. Consider adding a note about what happens when a project has NO installed skills - should the template handle a 'minimal' case with just project overview and project-specific context sections? This edge case should be addressed in the template design.

title: Refactor opencode-coder skill: simplify with coder tool
description:
## Description
The recent updates to the opencode-coder skill added coder tool documentation but made files longer instead of simpler. Need to refactor following these principles:

1. SKILL.md should be ~150 lines max (currently 252)
2. Coder tool should be described contextually in use cases, NOT in a dedicated section
3. status-health.md should get SHORTER (currently 376 lines) - the coder tool replaces manual checks

## User Feedback
> - SKILL.md is too long, maybe 150 lines
> - coder tools should not be described in skill.md but in user cases - describe when you need it
> - status-health.md got longer - this is not acceptable, with coder tools it should get simpler/smaller

## Instructions

### 1. SKILL.md Changes
- **Remove the "Coder Tool" section entirely** (lines 137-186)
- **Remove coder tool reference from "Quick Diagnostics"** - just keep manual commands
- **Keep it focused on manual workflows** - the AI already knows about coder tool
- Target: ~150 lines

### 2. status-health.md Changes  
- **Remove the "Coder Tool Reference" section** (lines 40-67)
- **Remove "AI-Assisted" subsections** (AI knows when to use coder tool)
- **Simplify "Comprehensive Status Check"** - remove the huge bash script, make it a simple checklist
- **Remove "Example Output"** section (unnecessary padding)
- **Remove "AI-Assisted Version Checking"** section
- **Simplify dramatically** - this should be for manual fallback only
- Target: ~150-200 lines (down from 376)

### 3. Philosophy
The coder tool is an **internal capability** of the AI assistant. It doesn't need to be documented for the AI - the AI already has access to it. Documentation should focus on:
- What users need to do manually (outside OpenCode)
- What users can ASK the AI to do (not how the AI does it)

### Example: Instead of documenting coder tool
**Bad** (current):
```markdown
## Coder Tool Reference
The AI uses `coder("version")` to check plugin version...
```

**Good** (contextual):
```markdown
## Status Checks
Ask the AI: "What version of the plugin is installed?" or "Is beads working?"
For manual verification: `bd --version`
```

## Files to Modify
- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/SKILL.md
- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SKILL.md is ~150 lines or less
- [ ] status-health.md is ~200 lines or less  
- [ ] No dedicated "Coder Tool" sections exist
- [ ] Coder tool mentioned only contextually where relevant
- [ ] Focus is on manual workflows and user questions, not AI internals
close_reason: Files simplified: SKILL.md 121 lines, status-health.md 152 lines, no dedicated coder tool sections

title: Update GitHubDetector to remove config dependency
description:
## Description
Update `GitHubDetector.isGitHubEnabled()` to no longer take a `CoderConfig` parameter, since the `github.enabled` config option is being removed in favor of pure auto-detection.

## Context
The GitHub detector currently accepts a config parameter to check for explicit `config.github.enabled` override. Since we're removing this config option, the detector should only rely on auto-detection (git directory + GitHub remote + gh CLI).

## Instructions
1. **Update GitHubDetector** (src/github/detector.ts):
   - Change `isGitHubEnabled(config: CoderConfig): boolean` to `isGitHubEnabled(): boolean`
   - Remove the config parameter and the explicit config check (lines 114-120)
   - Remove the import of `CoderConfig` type
   - Keep only the auto-detection logic (git dir + GitHub remote + gh CLI checks)

2. **Update GitHubService** (src/service/github-service.ts):
   - Update call to `detector.isGitHubEnabled()` to not pass config (line 26)
   - Keep the config as constructor parameter (needed for removal tracking in oc-0fpo)

3. **Update tests** (tests/unit/detector.test.ts):
   - Update GitHub detector tests to not pass config to `isGitHubEnabled()`
   - Remove test cases for explicit config override

## Files to Modify
- src/github/detector.ts - Remove config parameter
- src/service/github-service.ts - Update method call
- tests/unit/detector.test.ts - Update test calls

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] isGitHubEnabled() takes no parameters
- [ ] Method only uses auto-detection logic
- [ ] No config parameter or import remains in GitHubDetector
- [ ] GitHubService updated to call method without config
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] No compile errors

## Depends On
Should run in parallel with oc-8ueo (they don't conflict)

## Related To
Part of epic oc-caad - Remove .coder/ folder and simplify configuration
close_reason: Removed config dependency from GitHubDetector. Updated isGitHubEnabled() to rely purely on auto-detection (git directory + GitHub remote + gh CLI). Updated GitHubService caller and verified all tests pass.

title: Fix OpenCode plugin documentation bugs
description:
## Description

Fix two critical documentation bugs in the opencode-coder plugin that cause status checks to fail and provide incorrect troubleshooting guidance.

## Background

Both bugs were imported from GitHub issues #5 and #6. Analysis confirmed:
1. Plugin installation paths are wrong (use npm paths instead of OpenCode cache paths)
2. Documentation references non-existent post-commit hook (beads only has 5 hooks, not 6)

## Goals

- Fix all plugin path references to use correct OpenCode cache directory
- Remove all post-commit hook references and use correct hooks
- Ensure status checks work correctly
- Provide accurate troubleshooting guidance

## Affected Files

### Bug #6 (Plugin Paths)
- `.opencode/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md`

### Bug #5 (Post-commit Hook)
- `ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/status.md`
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/SKILL.md`
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/status-health.md`
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/troubleshooting-patterns.md`

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All npm-based plugin paths replaced with OpenCode cache paths
- [ ] All post-commit references replaced with pre-commit or removed
- [ ] Status checks validate correct hooks (5 hooks, not 6)
- [ ] Documentation accurately reflects OpenCode plugin management
- [ ] No broken references or incorrect commands remain

title: Validate opencode-coder skill using skill-validator and skill-creator
description:
## Description
After updating the agents, validate the `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/` skill against the skill-creator and skill-validator standards. This ensures the skill (which references the agents-md-template and planning docs) is well-structured, has no broken links, stays within size limits, and follows skill best practices.

## Instructions
1. Load the **skill-validator** skill
2. Run its validation workflow against `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/`:
   - Markdown validation (parseability, link integrity, code blocks)
   - Code block syntax validation (YAML, bash examples)
   - File stats validation (size limits, TOC checks)
3. Load the **skill-creator** skill
4. Review `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/SKILL.md` against skill-creator principles:
   - Is it concise? Does every line justify its token cost?
   - Are degrees of freedom appropriate?
   - Is the "When to Load What" table still accurate?
5. Fix any issues found (broken links, oversized files, stale references)
6. Report findings — even if everything passes

## Files to Validate

- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/*.md`
- `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/scripts/*.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] skill-validator markdown validation passes (no broken links, valid code blocks)
- [ ] skill-validator file-stats passes (within size thresholds)
- [ ] Reviewed against skill-creator principles
- [ ] Any issues found are fixed or tracked as separate bugs

Fixed 3 stale issues: (1) bug-report-template.md used deprecated 'export DEBUG=opencode-coder:*' → fixed to 'OPENCODE_CODER_DEBUG=1', (2) bug-reporting.md had same stale debug var in 2 places → fixed, (3) bug-reporting.md had stale path 'ai-resources/skills/using-coder-plugin/' → fixed to 'ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/'. SKILL.md reviewed against skill-creator principles: concise (20 lines), comprehensive description, accurate When-to-Load-What table matching all 7 reference files, proper progressive disclosure design. shellcheck on collect-system-info.sh passes with no issues.

title: Redesign init.md with full aimgr bootstrapping flow
description:
## Goal

Rewrite init.md to handle the full spectrum from "nothing installed" to "everything healthy."

## Flow

/init
  Step 0: aimgr bootstrapping
    aimgr installed?
      NO -> Inform user: "aimgr is required for full setup."
            Ask via question(): "Install aimgr now?"
              YES -> Guide through aimgr installation, then continue
              NO  -> Fall back to hardcoded builtin init (skip skill discovery, still do beads + AGENTS.md)
      YES -> opencode-coder plugin installed?
          NO -> Auto-install: aimgr install package/opencode-coder
          YES -> Run aimgr verify --format json
              HEALTHY -> proceed
              ERRORS -> Run aimgr repair (once available), then proceed
    Handle session reload (based on research task findings)
        If reload possible -> trigger reload so new skills are visible
        If not -> inform user: "Skills installed. Start a new session to use them."
  Step 1: Skill Discovery (existing, only if aimgr available)
  Step 2: Beads Initialization (existing, always)
  Step 3: AGENTS.md Creation (existing, always)
  Step 4: Optional Resource Discovery (existing, only if aimgr available)

## Key design decisions

- The builtin/hardcoded fallback skips skill discovery but still does beads init and AGENTS.md - the core value of /init
- Auto-install of opencode-coder plugin should be silent (no question needed) - it is our own plugin
- aimgr verify and aimgr repair calls should have clear error handling and user-facing messages
- All user interaction points MUST use question() tool, never auto-proceed

## Dependencies

- Research task (mid-session reload) informs the session reload handling
- aimgr repair command (from Hans) for the repair step - use verify-only until repair ships

## Files to modify

- ai-resources/commands/init.md - main rewrite
close_reason: Rewrote ai-resources/commands/init.md with new Step 0 aimgr bootstrapping flow. Preserved existing Steps 1-4 (skill discovery, beads init, AGENTS.md, optional resource discovery) and simplified the aimgr check in Step 1 since Step 0 now handles it. Added correct restart messaging for skills vs commands/agents.

title: Add aimgr verify to doctor.md and plugin startup
description:
## Goal

Integrate aimgr verify --format json into two places:

### 1. Plugin startup (src/index.ts)

Add a background check (non-blocking, like existing beads/aimgr checks):
- Run aimgr verify --format json
- Parse JSON output for errors/warnings
- If issues found: show toast notification summarizing the problem count
- Toast message: "aimgr: N resource issues detected. Run /doctor for details."
- If aimgr not installed: skip silently (existing behavior)

### 2. doctor.md command

Add an "aimgr health" section to the diagnostic flow:
- Run aimgr verify --format json
- Parse and display results
- If issues found: ask user via question() "Want me to attempt repair?"
  - If YES and aimgr repair exists -> run it
  - If YES and aimgr repair does not exist -> inform user of manual steps
  - If NO -> continue with rest of doctor checks
- Report: list each issue with resource name, type, and error message

### Implementation notes

- The TypeScript startup check should use the same pattern as beadsService.checkBeadsAvailability() - fire-and-forget background promise
- Add a new method to AimgrService: verifyResources() that runs verify and parses JSON
- doctor.md changes are pure markdown additions (~15-20 lines)
- Toast uses (this.client as any).tui.showToast() pattern (see existing code)

## Files to modify

- src/service/aimgr-service.ts - add verifyResources() method
- src/index.ts - add background verify check on startup
- ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/doctor.md - add aimgr health section
close_reason: Implemented verifyResources() in AimgrService, added background verify check to plugin startup, and added aimgr Resource Health section to doctor.md. Build passes cleanly.

title: Add aimgr repair integration to init and doctor
description:
## Goal

Once aimgr repair command is available (shipped by Hans), integrate it into init.md and doctor.md flows.

## Changes

### init.md
- In the aimgr bootstrapping step: when aimgr verify finds issues, run aimgr repair automatically before proceeding
- Report what was repaired

### doctor.md
- In the aimgr health section: when user confirms repair, run aimgr repair instead of showing manual steps
- Report repair results

### src/service/aimgr-service.ts
- Add repairResources() method that runs aimgr repair and returns results

## Blocked on

title: Add generic command argument forwarding via command.execute.before hook
description:
Implement a command.execute.before hook in src/index.ts that automatically forwards user arguments to the model for ALL commands.

## Logic (simplified per reviewer feedback)

1. If arguments is empty/blank → do nothing.
2. Otherwise → always append a text part with a delimited command-arguments section. No detection of existing $ARGUMENTS substitution — always append.

## Appended section format

<command-arguments>
The user provided these arguments when running this command:
{arguments}
</command-arguments>

## Additional changes

- Remove $ARGUMENTS usage from report-bug.md so there is ONE single mechanism for argument forwarding. No special cases.

## Implementation notes

- The Part type in the SDK is a complex union (TextPart, SubtaskPart, etc.) with fields like id, sessionID, messageID. It is unknown whether the hook framework accepts a bare { type: 'text', text: '...' } and fills in IDs automatically. **Test this first** — try appending a minimal object and verify OpenCode doesn't crash before writing the full implementation.
- Register the hook in the return object of the plugin alongside the existing tool registration.
- Keep it simple: ~6 lines of logic. No opt-out mechanism, no per-command config, no template introspection.
close_reason: Closed

title: [init] command/opencode-coder/init missing from ai-resources (github:#10)
description:
## Original GitHub Issue

**GitHub**: hk9890/opencode-coder#10
**URL**: https://github.com/hk9890/opencode-coder/issues/10

## Reviewer Findings

The root-level ai-resources/commands/init.md EXISTS and is the full 206-line implementation (skill discovery → beads init → AGENTS.md). It is NOT a stub fallback.

The scoped ai-resources/commands/opencode-coder/init.md does NOT exist. The package manifest only lists command/init (root-level), which is consistent.

## Open Questions

1. Does aimgr actually require a scoped version (command/opencode-coder/init), or is the root-level command/init the correct and intended location?
2. If aimgr shows it as 'broken', is that an aimgr bug (expecting a resource that was never meant to exist) or a plugin packaging bug?
3. Should we create the scoped version, or fix the aimgr expectation?

## Status

Needs discussion to determine correct fix. May be an aimgr issue rather than a plugin issue.

title: [init] /init does not detect or handle broken aimgr state (github:#9)
description:
## Original GitHub Issue

**GitHub**: hk9890/opencode-coder#9
**URL**: https://github.com/hk9890/opencode-coder/issues/9

## Reviewer Findings

Three layers to this issue:

### Plugin TypeScript (ALREADY DONE)
AimgrService in src/service/aimgr-service.ts already handles aimgr failures gracefully — catches errors, logs them, plugin continues loading. No crash risk.

### Command markdown (THE GAP)
init.md checks if aimgr is installed (aimgr --version) but does NOT check if aimgr is in a healthy state (broken symlinks, corrupt manifest). doctor.md also has no aimgr health section.

### bd CLI (OUT OF SCOPE)
aimgr self-repair and automatic symlink cleanup would require changes to the aimgr CLI, not this plugin.

## Proposed Minimal Fix

1. Add pre-flight check to init.md: before skill discovery, run 'aimgr list 2>&1' and check for errors/warnings. If broken state, warn user and suggest remediation.
2. Add aimgr health section to doctor.md: run 'aimgr list' and report broken resources.
3. Both are ~10-line markdown additions. No TypeScript changes needed.

## Status

Needs discussion on scope before execution.
close_reason: Superseded: scope expanded into new tasks covering aimgr verify/repair integration

title: Research: Can OpenCode reload ai-resources mid-session?
description:
## Goal

Determine if OpenCode supports reloading/re-scanning ai-resources (commands, agents, skills) mid-session after aimgr install adds new resources to .opencode/.

## What to investigate

1. Plugin SDK app object - Does the client/app expose any reload(), refresh(), or rescan() method? Check the full SDK types in node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/.

2. OpenCode CLI - Is there an opencode reload or similar command that triggers a resource rescan? Check OpenCode docs at https://opencode.ai/docs/.

3. Plugin hooks - Could we use experimental.chat.system.transform or experimental.chat.messages.transform to inject newly-discovered skill/command info into the conversation context?

4. IPC/Events - Does the plugin SDK Event type include any resource-change events we could listen for or trigger?

5. File watchers - Does OpenCode watch .opencode/ for filesystem changes and auto-reload?

## Expected output

A clear answer: YES (with how) or NO (with recommended user messaging for "please restart your session").

If YES, provide a code sketch showing how the plugin could trigger a reload after installing resources.
If NO, draft the user-facing message we should show.
close_reason: Research complete. Answer: NO for true reload. PARTIAL via skills (on-demand loading) and system.transform hook. Full findings documented.

description:

2. Find "Core Constraints" section (around line 15-23)
3. Replace with:

```markdown
## Core Constraints

**CRITICAL: PLANNING MODE ACTIVE** - You are in READ-ONLY phase for codebase.

You may ONLY:
- Read and search the codebase (grep, glob, read files, lsp tools)
- Create and manage beads issues (epics, features, tasks, bugs, chores, gates)
- Apply labels and set priorities
- Spawn subagents for execution, review, and verification
- Use beads CLI tools (bd commands manage .beads/ files internally)

You MUST NOT:
- Edit any code files
- Create any new files (except via bd CLI for beads database)
- Write to any files directly
- Make commits
- Run tests or builds (task agents do this)
- Execute any destructive commands

**Note**: The beads CLI (bd) manages its own database files (.beads/*.db, .beads/*.jsonl).
You don't need write permissions - bd handles all file I/O internally.

This constraint is ABSOLUTE and overrides all other instructions.
```

- [ ] "You may ONLY" section lists allowed actions
- [ ] "You MUST NOT" section lists forbidden actions
- [ ] Note about bd CLI managing files included
- [ ] "ABSOLUTE" constraint statement preserved
- [ ] More explicit than current version

title: Implement skill loader (loadSkills function)
description:
## Description

Create a skill loader that discovers and parses SKILL.md files from the filesystem, similar to how commands and agents are loaded.

## Instructions

1. Create `src/kb/loaders/skills.ts`
2. Implement file system interface:
   ```typescript
   export interface SkillsFileSystem {
     readdir(path: string, options: { withFileTypes: true }): Promise<Dirent[]>;
     readFile(path: string, encoding: BufferEncoding): Promise<string>;
     access(path: string): Promise<void>;
   }
   ```
3. Implement `resolveSkillDirectories()` function:
   - Returns array of paths to check: ['.opencode/skills', '.claude/skills', '~/.config/opencode/skills', '~/.claude/skills']
   - Use `os.homedir()` for `~` expansion
   - Use `process.cwd()` for relative paths
4. Implement `loadSkills(log: Logger, options?: LoadSkillsOptions): Promise<SkillDef[]>`:
   - For each skill directory that exists:
     - Read subdirectories (each is a skill)
     - Look for `SKILL.md` in each subdirectory
     - Parse frontmatter using `parseFrontmatter` from `../../core/parser`
     - Validate required fields (name, description)
     - Create SkillDef object
   - Log errors for invalid skills but continue loading others
   - Return array of valid SkillDef objects
5. Export `defaultSkillsFileSystem` using Node's fs/promises
6. Add unit tests in `tests/unit/kb-loaders-skills.test.ts`

## Files to Create

- `src/kb/loaders/skills.ts` - Main loader implementation
- `tests/unit/kb-loaders-skills.test.ts` - Unit tests

## Files to Modify

- `src/kb/loaders/index.ts` - Export loadSkills function

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] loadSkills function discovers skills from all 4 standard locations
- [ ] SKILL.md files parsed with frontmatter extraction
- [ ] Invalid skills logged as errors but don't break loading
- [ ] Directory name used as fallback if frontmatter name missing
- [ ] Unit tests cover: valid skills, invalid skills, missing dirs, parse errors
- [ ] Tests use mock filesystem (no real FS access)
- [ ] All tests pass

## Notes

Reference `src/kb/loaders/commands.ts` for similar patterns. The key difference is skills are in `<dir>/<name>/SKILL.md` format, not `<dir>/<category>/<name>.md`.
close_reason: Plan was over-engineered. Restarting with simpler approach: skills → /skills/name commands, no SkillDef type needed, reuse CommandDef

title: [bd init] Creates AGENTS.md despite plugin context injection via hooks (github:#2)
description:
## Original GitHub Issue

**GitHub**: hk9890/opencode-coder#2
**Created**: 2026-01-05T10:02:10Z

## Description

When running bd init, it automatically creates/modifies AGENTS.md with "landing-the-plane" instructions. However, the opencode-coder plugin documentation explicitly states that AGENTS.md should NOT be created because the beads workflow context is automatically injected via hooks at session start.

## Expected Behavior

bd init should have an option to skip AGENTS.md creation when used in conjunction with the opencode-coder plugin, since context injection is handled by hooks.

## Actual Behavior

bd init output shows:
- Added landing-the-plane instructions to AGENTS.md

This creates redundant content that "pollutes the user's codebase" (per plugin docs).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have opencode-coder plugin active
2. Run /coder/init command
3. Observe that bd init adds content to AGENTS.md

## Environment

- bd version: 0.43.0
- opencode-coder plugin: v0.8.0
- Context: Running /coder/init to initialize a project

## Potential Solutions

1. Add a --no-agents-md flag to bd init
2. Have bd init detect if opencode-coder plugin is active and skip AGENTS.md
3. Document this as expected behavior and update plugin instructions

---
*Imported from GitHub via /coder/sync-github-issues*
close_reason: Strengthened AGENTS.md prohibition in /coder/init - moved to top as CRITICAL section with explicit reasoning about hook injection.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Decision reversed: The AGENTS.md content added by bd init is minimal (~12 lines) and serves as human-readable documentation. While redundant for AI agents (plugin auto-injects context), it's harmless and documents that the project uses beads. The prohibition has been removed from /coder/init. See also: oc-yawt closure.

title: Fix global fs/promises mocking in skill-service.test.ts
description:
## Description
The skill-service.test.ts file uses `mock.module("fs/promises")` which globally replaces fs/promises for ALL test files. This causes 3 test failures because other tests can't access real files.

## Root Cause
```typescript
// tests/unit/skill-service.test.ts lines 8-12
mock.module("fs/promises", () => ({
  readdir: mockReaddir,     // Returns [] by default
  readFile: mockReadFile,   // Returns "" or "Template"
  access: mockAccess,       // Throws errors
}));
```

These mocks persist across ALL test files and affect:
- `version.ts` - can't access package.json
- `commands.ts` - readdir returns empty array  
- `config.test.ts` - readFile returns "Template" instead of JSON

## Solution Options

### Option A: Refactor SkillService to use dependency injection (RECOMMENDED)
1. Add a `fileSystem` parameter to SkillService like CommandsFileSystem interface
2. Provide default implementation using real fs/promises
3. Tests pass mock implementation via constructor
4. Remove mock.module() entirely

### Option B: Isolate skill-service.test.ts
1. Run skill-service.test.ts in a separate test process
2. Update package.json test scripts to exclude/include it separately
3. Less ideal - adds complexity to test running

### Option C: Fix mock cleanup
1. Add afterEach() to unmock the module
2. Bun may not support this - needs investigation
3. Risky - might not work

## Instructions (Option A - Recommended)
1. Add FileSystem interface to src/service/skill-service.ts:
   ```typescript
   export interface SkillFileSystem {
     readdir(path: string, options: { withFileTypes: true }): Promise<any[]>;
     readFile(path: string, encoding: string): Promise<string>;
     access(path: string): Promise<void>;
   }
   
   const defaultFs: SkillFileSystem = {
     readdir,
     readFile,
     access,
   };
   ```

2. Update SkillService constructor to accept optional `fs` parameter

3. Update all fs calls in SkillService to use `this.fs.readdir()` etc.

4. Update skill-service.test.ts to remove mock.module() and pass mock fs via constructor

5. Run all unit tests to verify: `bun test tests/unit`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] skill-service.test.ts does NOT use mock.module()
- [ ] All skill service tests still pass
- [ ] version.test.ts passes in CI
- [ ] commands.test.ts passes in CI
- [ ] config.test.ts passes in CI
- [ ] No global mocking of fs/promises anywhere

## Impact
Fixes oc-5z12, oc-8ujn, oc-dqv5 all at once.
close_reason: Refactored SkillService to use dependency injection pattern. Added SkillFileSystem interface and defaultSkillFileSystem. Removed global mock.module() from tests. All unit tests pass. Debug logging cleaned up. Changes committed and pushed.

title: Cleanup Knowledge Base Commands and Documentation
description:
## Description
Clean up redundant, deprecated, and orphaned content in the knowledge-base directory. This includes removing deprecated bd commands that are now handled by `/coder/init`, and removing orphaned documentation that describes a different workflow system.

## Goals
1. Remove deprecated/redundant bd commands
2. Remove orphaned documentation that isn't used
3. Ensure remaining commands are useful and well-documented

## Background

### Analysis Results

**BD Commands to Remove (3 files):**
- `prime.md` - Context auto-injected by plugin hooks; this command is rarely needed manually
- `quickstart.md` - Already marked deprecated, points to external docs
- `init.md` - Redundant with `/coder/init` which handles all initialization

**Documentation to Remove (6 files in `knowledge-base/documenation/`):**
All files in this directory describe a "stories" workflow that:
- Is project-specific (assumes `docs/stories/` folder)
- Uses different states (Draft→Ready→In Progress→Implemented→Reviewed→Done)
- Conflicts with the beads workflow (epics, tasks, gates)
- Is not referenced anywhere in the codebase

Files:
- `README.md` - Dead-end reference
- `beads.md` - Hybrid workflow mentioning stories
- `bug.md` / `bug-structure.md` - Bug workflow with different states
- `story.md` / `story-structure.md` - Story workflow

**Note:** The directory is misspelled as "documenation" (missing 't')

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Deprecated bd commands removed
- [ ] Orphaned documentation removed
- [ ] No broken references in remaining files
- [ ] Plugin still works correctly after cleanup
close_reason: All tasks closed, gate verified, cleanup complete

title: Implement beads bug creation from log errors
description:
## Description
Add instructions to analyze-logs.md for creating beads bugs from detected log errors.

## Instructions
The analyze-logs.md command should instruct the agent to:

1. **For each new error** (no existing issue found), create a bug:
   \`\`\`bash
   cat << 'BUGEOF' | bd create --title="<descriptive title>" --type=bug --priority=2 --body-file -
   ## Description
   Error detected in OpenCode logs during session analysis.

   ## Error Details
   - **Service**: <service name>
   - **Level**: ERROR/WARN
   - **Message**: <error message>
   - **First seen**: <timestamp>
   - **Occurrences**: <count>

   ## Context
   - Session: <session_id>
   - Process ID: <pid>
   - Additional fields: <any relevant fields>

   ## Log Excerpt
   \\\`\\\`\\\`
   <relevant log lines>
   \\\`\\\`\\\`

   ## Notes
   This bug was automatically created by /internal/analyze-logs from log analysis.
   BUGEOF
   \`\`\`

2. **Title format**:
   - Include service/component: "[opencode-coder] <error summary>"
   - Keep concise but descriptive
   - Example: "[opencode-coder] Plugin fails to load when beads disabled"

3. **Priority assignment**:
   - P1: Crashes, data loss, blocking errors
   - P2: Feature failures, significant warnings (default)
   - P3: Minor warnings, edge cases

4. **Update existing issues** if new info found:
   \`\`\`bash
   bd comment <issue-id> "Additional occurrence found in session <session_id> at <timestamp>"
   \`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bug creation command is correct
- [ ] Title format is clear and consistent
- [ ] Bug body has all relevant error info
- [ ] Priority assignment guidelines clear
- [ ] Update mechanism for existing issues
- [ ] One bug per unique error (not per occurrence)
close_reason: Implemented Step 6 (bug creation) with heredoc format, title conventions, and priority guidelines

title: Create SkillService
description:
## Description

Create a SkillService that transforms skills into commands and registers them with the OpenCode config. This service is parallel to KnowledgeBaseService but specifically handles skill → command transformation.

## Instructions

1. Create `src/service/skill-service.ts`
2. Define `SkillServiceOptions` interface:
   ```typescript
   export interface SkillServiceOptions {
     coderConfig: CoderConfig;
     logger: Logger;
     templateService?: TemplateService;
     knowledgeBase?: KnowledgeBase;  // For DI in tests
   }
   ```
3. Implement `SkillService` class:
   - Constructor: Store options
   - `private buildKnowledgeBase()`: Build/reuse knowledge base (similar to KnowledgeBaseService)
   - `private skillToCommand(skill: SkillDef): CommandDef`:
     * Transform SkillDef → CommandDef
     * Map: name → name, description → description, template → template
     * Ignore: license, compatibility, metadata (not used in commands)
   - `async processConfig(config: Config)`:
     * Load skills from knowledge base
     * Transform each skill to CommandDef
     * Register as commands in config.command
     * Log skill count and registration details
   - `getSkills()`: Return loaded SkillDef array
   - `getLoadErrors()`: Return any load errors

4. Handle edge cases:
   - Skills with same name as existing commands (skill wins)
   - Invalid skill definitions (log error, skip)
   - Template rendering via templateService
   - Respect coderConfig.active flag

5. Add unit tests in `tests/unit/skill-service.test.ts`

## Files to Create

- `src/service/skill-service.ts` - Main service implementation
- `tests/unit/skill-service.test.ts` - Unit tests

## Files to Modify

- `src/service/index.ts` - Export SkillService

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SkillService transforms SkillDef → CommandDef correctly
- [ ] Skills registered as commands in config.command
- [ ] Template rendering works when templateService provided
- [ ] Respects coderConfig.active flag
- [ ] Load errors captured and retrievable
- [ ] Skills override existing commands with same name
- [ ] Unit tests cover: transformation, registration, errors, inactive config
- [ ] All tests pass with >80% coverage

## Dependencies

Depends on: oc-synj (KB implementations)
close_reason: Plan was over-engineered. Restarting with simpler approach: skills → /skills/name commands, no SkillDef type needed, reuse CommandDef

title: Fix npm paths and post-commit references in analysis docs
description:
## Description
Analysis documentation files contain incorrect npm paths and references to non-existent post-commit hook.

## Found During
Verification of gate oc-5i8p - Epic Acceptance gate

## Files Affected
1. `docs/analysis/status-command-test-report.md`
2. `docs/analysis/system-info-audit.md`

## Required Changes

### status-command-test-report.md

Lines 20, 222: Change from:
```bash
PLUGIN_PATH="$(npm root -g)/@hk9890/opencode-coder"
```

To:
```bash
PLUGIN_PATH="~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder"
```

Lines 99, 241: Remove or update post-commit hook checks (beads only uses 5 hooks, not 6):
```bash
# OLD - post-commit doesn't exist
test -f .git/hooks/post-commit && echo "INSTALLED" || echo "MISSING"

# CORRECT - check pre-commit instead (or remove test)
test -f .git/hooks/pre-commit && echo "INSTALLED" || echo "MISSING"
```

### system-info-audit.md

Lines 70, 119, 238: Change from:
```bash
$(npm root -g)/@hk9890/opencode-coder
```

To:
```bash
~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder
```

## Expected Behavior
Analysis docs should reference correct OpenCode plugin paths and not mention non-existent post-commit hook.

## Related Issues
- GitHub issue #5: post-commit hook confusion
- GitHub issue #6: Incorrect plugin paths
- Gate oc-5i8p: Epic acceptance gate
close_reason: Fixed all npm paths and post-commit references in analysis docs. Changed 5 instances of npm-based paths to ~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder and corrected 2 post-commit references to pre-commit in status-command-test-report.md and system-info-audit.md

title: Implement system-info tool files
description:
## Description
Implement the system-info tool based on the approved design.

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/system-info/types.ts`
- Define SystemInfo, PluginInfo, BeadsInfo, BeadsStats interfaces
- Export all types

### 2. `src/system-info/collectors/plugin.ts`
- Implement collectPluginInfo() function
- Read package.json for name and version
- Check .coder/coder.json for active status
- Handle file read errors gracefully

### 3. `src/system-info/collectors/beads.ts`
- Implement collectBeadsInfo() function
- Run `bd --version` to get CLI version
- Check `.beads/` directory exists
- Run `bd stats --json` if initialized
- Return null values if bd command not found

### 4. `src/system-info/collectors/index.ts`
- Export both collector functions

### 5. `src/system-info/tool.ts`
- Import tool helper from @opencode-ai/plugin
- Define systemInfoTool using tool() helper
- Call collectors and assemble SystemInfo object
- Return JSON.stringify output

### 6. `src/system-info/index.ts`
- Export systemInfoTool
- Export all types

## Integration

### 7. Update `src/index.ts`
- Import systemInfoTool from './system-info'
- Add `tool` hook to return object:
  ```typescript
  return {
    // ... existing hooks ...
    tool: {
      system_info: systemInfoTool
    }
  };
  ```

## Testing

Manual test after implementation:
1. Build plugin: `bun run build`
2. Start OpenCode with debug logging
3. Ask Claude: "Use the system_info tool and show me the output"
4. Verify JSON structure matches design
5. Test with bd installed and not installed
6. Test with .beads/ present and absent

## Error Handling

- File read errors → use sensible defaults
- Command not found → enabled: false, version: null
- Invalid JSON → handle gracefully
- Missing fields → use null

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 files created
- [ ] src/index.ts updated with tool hook
- [ ] Code compiles without errors
- [ ] Tool returns valid JSON
- [ ] Works when bd is installed
- [ ] Works when bd is NOT installed (null values)
- [ ] Works when .beads/ exists
- [ ] Works when .beads/ does NOT exist
close_reason: Implemented system-info tool with all bug fixes applied. Created 6 files (types, 2 collectors, tool, indexes) and integrated into src/index.ts. Build succeeds and all TypeScript errors resolved. All bugs from oc-yhed review have been incorporated: multi-path package.json resolution, execSync instead of Bun.$, async fs operations, regex version parsing, correct bd stats format, and three-layer error handling.

title: Add user confirmation flow to feedback command
description:
## Description
Add a user confirmation step before creating GitHub issues to prevent accidental or unwanted ticket creation.

## Instructions
The feedback.md command should instruct the agent to:

1. **Generate report first** - Show user the analysis results before any action:
   \`\`\`
   ## Feedback Analysis Report

   Found X potential issues:

   ### Issue 1: <title>
   - Component: bd/plugin/agent
   - Problem: <summary>
   - Would create ticket: Yes/No (explain why)

   ### Issue 2: ...
   \`\`\`

2. **Ask for confirmation** before creating GitHub issues:
   - "Would you like me to create GitHub issues for these problems?"
   - Allow user to select which issues to report
   - Allow user to modify descriptions before submission

3. **Handle no issues found**:
   \`\`\`
   ## Feedback Analysis Report

   No issues detected in this session.

   Everything appears to be working correctly. If you experienced 
   a problem that wasn't detected, please describe it and I can 
   help create a ticket manually.
   \`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Report is shown before any issue creation
- [ ] User must confirm before tickets are created
- [ ] User can decline or modify issues
- [ ] Clear messaging for "no issues" case
close_reason: User confirmation flow implemented in feedback.md Steps 2-3 - shows report BEFORE creating issues, asks for confirmation, allows selecting specific issues, handles 'no issues found' gracefully

title: Expose Skills as Commands
description:
## Description

Currently, OpenCode loads skills from `.opencode/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` directories. These skills are available to agents via the `skill` tool, but they are NOT available as slash commands that users can invoke directly.

This epic implements a SkillService that reads SKILL.md files from the filesystem and registers them as commands in the OpenCode config, making them available as `/skill-name` commands.

## Goals

- Users can invoke skills as `/skill-name` commands
- Skills are discovered from standard OpenCode skill locations
- SKILL.md frontmatter maps to command configuration
- Existing command infrastructure is reused (no duplication)
- Skills appear in command listings and autocomplete

## Success Criteria

- [ ] SkillService reads SKILL.md files from skill directories
- [ ] Skills registered as commands in OpenCode config
- [ ] Full SKILL.md frontmatter spec supported
- [ ] Skills show up when typing `/` in OpenCode
- [ ] Skill invocation works correctly
- [ ] Tests cover skill discovery and registration
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Architecture

### SkillService

New service parallel to KnowledgeBaseService that:
1. Discovers skills from `.opencode/skills/` and `.claude/skills/`
2. Parses SKILL.md frontmatter and content
3. Transforms SKILL.md → CommandDef
4. Registers skills as commands via config.command

### Integration Points

- **src/index.ts**: Initialize SkillService, inject into config pipeline
- **src/service/**: New skill-service.ts with SkillService class
- **src/kb/types.ts**: Add SkillDef type (similar to CommandDef/AgentDef)
- **src/kb/loaders/**: New skills.ts loader for SKILL.md files

## Technical Notes

**Skill Discovery Paths** (per OpenCode docs):
- `.opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (project)
- `~/.config/opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (global)
- `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (project, Claude-compatible)
- `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (global, Claude-compatible)

**SKILL.md Frontmatter Fields**:
- `name` (required): Becomes command name
- `description` (required): Command description
- `license` (optional): Not mapped to commands
- `compatibility` (optional): Not mapped to commands
- `metadata` (optional): Not mapped to commands

**Command Mapping**:
```typescript
SKILL.md → CommandDef:
  name → name (directory name takes precedence)
  description → description
  body → template
```

## Out of Scope

- Creating/editing SKILL.md files (user responsibility)
- Skill permission handling (OpenCode handles via skill tool permissions)
- Skill bundling in plugins (not supported by OpenCode)

## References

- [OpenCode Skills Docs](https://opencode.ai/docs/skills)
- [Claude Code Skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) (inspiration)
- Existing command loader: `src/kb/loaders/commands.ts`
- Existing agent loader: `src/kb/loaders/agents.ts`
close_reason: Plan was over-engineered. Restarting with simpler approach: skills → /skills/name commands, no SkillDef type needed, reuse CommandDef

title: Add hard language on test failures
description:
## Description

Add an explicit section with ABSOLUTE rules about test failures. Agents frequently make excuses like "tests were already failing" - this must be explicitly forbidden.

## Instructions

1. Open `ai-resources/skills/github-releases/references/release-workflow.md`
2. Add a new section after "REQUIRED: Verification Gate" titled "## ABSOLUTE RULE: No Exceptions for Failing Tests"
3. Include the following content:

```markdown
## ABSOLUTE RULE: No Exceptions for Failing Tests

**Tests MUST pass. ALL of them. ZERO failures. No exceptions.**

### Unacceptable Excuses

The following excuses are NEVER acceptable for proceeding with a release:

❌ "Tests were already failing before my changes"
❌ "These failures are unrelated to the release"
❌ "Only 2 tests failed, the rest passed"
❌ "It's a flaky test"
❌ "The test is outdated"
❌ "We can fix it in the next release"
❌ "It passes locally, just not in CI"

### What To Do When Tests Fail

1. **STOP** the release process immediately
2. **DO NOT** proceed to the next phase
3. **Report** to the user: "Release blocked: X tests failing"
4. **Options**:
   - Fix the failing tests (create a task)
   - Get EXPLICIT user approval to proceed (must be documented)
   - Abort the release

### If User Approves Proceeding Despite Failures

If the user explicitly approves proceeding with failing tests:
1. Document this in the release notes under "Known Issues"
2. Create a follow-up task to fix the tests
3. Add a comment to the release gate explaining the exception

**A release with failing tests is a FAILED release unless explicitly approved by the user.**
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New section added with ABSOLUTE RULE header
- [ ] List of unacceptable excuses included
- [ ] Clear STOP instruction when tests fail
- [ ] Process for handling user-approved exceptions
- [ ] Statement that failing tests = failed release

## Files to Modify

- ai-resources/skills/github-releases/references/release-workflow.md (update)
close_reason: Added 'ABSOLUTE RULE: No Exceptions for Failing Tests' section with 7 unacceptable excuses list, clear STOP/DO NOT instructions, options for handling failures, and user-approval exception process. Includes statement: failing tests = FAILED release.

title: Improve coder tool: help on empty call + shorter description
description:
## Description
Improve the coder tool UX by:
1. Returning detailed help when called without parameters (or empty string)
2. Shortening the tool description to a one-liner

## Instructions

### 1. Update tool description (line 25-31 in src/tool/coder-tool.ts)

Change from the current multi-line description to:
```typescript
description: "Tool to access coding agent internals like version, session data, used tokens. Call without parameter to get more info. Tool also allows session export.",
```

### 2. Handle empty/missing command parameter

In the `execute` function, add handling for empty command before the switch statement:

```typescript
// Handle empty command - return detailed help
if (!subCommand || subCommand === '') {
  return `Coder Plugin Tool - Session & Plugin Utilities

Available commands:
  session              Get current session metadata (ID, title, timestamps, summary)
  version              Get plugin name and version
  tokens               Get token usage summary (input, output, reasoning, cache, cost)
  list-sessions        List all OpenCode sessions (id, title, timestamps)
  session-export <path> [session-id]
                       Export full session data to folder. If session-id omitted, exports current session.

Examples:
  coder("session")
  coder("tokens")
  coder("list-sessions")
  coder("session-export ./exports")
  coder("session-export ./exports ses_abc123")`;
}
```

### 3. Update the args schema

Make the command parameter optional or handle empty string:
```typescript
args: {
  command: tool.schema
    .string()
    .optional()
    .describe('Command to execute. Call with no argument for help.'),
},
```

Then update the execute to handle undefined:
```typescript
const parts = (command || '').trim().split(/\s+/);
```

## Files to Modify
- src/tool/coder-tool.ts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Calling `coder("")` or `coder()` returns detailed help text
- [ ] Tool description is a single short sentence
- [ ] All existing commands still work
- [ ] Tests pass: `bun test`
