SKILL.md contains Bidirectional Sync section (lines 164+) with high-level workflow and conflict detection overview. references/bidirectional-workflow.md fully implemented (512 lines) with GitHub-specific conflict detection, resolution commands, and integration with task-sync. All acceptance criteria met.

description:

error: expect(received).toBeGreaterThan(expected)

Received: 0

title: Test question tool in beads-planner-agent
description:
## Description
Manually test the question tool implementation to ensure it works correctly in beads-planner-agent with all the expected behaviors.

## Instructions
1. Ensure OpenCode is running in a beads-enabled project
2. Start a new chat session (beads-planner-agent should be default)
3. Run through each test scenario below

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Question tool is available
- Ask: "I want to add user authentication"
- Expected: Agent uses question tool (not just text) to ask about approach
- Verify: Question renders with options

### Scenario 2: Single question works
- Trigger agent to ask single choice question
- Expected: Question displays with options
- Select an option
- Verify: Agent receives answer and uses it in planning

### Scenario 3: Multiple questions work
- Trigger agent to ask multiple questions at once
- Expected: All questions display
- Answer all questions
- Verify: Agent receives all answers

### Scenario 4: Read-only enforcement
- Ask agent to directly edit a code file
- Expected: Agent cannot use edit/write tools (permission denied)
- Verify: Agent explains it cannot edit code

### Scenario 5: BD commands work
- Trigger agent to create beads issues
- Expected: Agent can run bd commands without approval prompts
- Verify: bd create, bd update, etc. work smoothly

### Scenario 6: Bash commands work
- Ask agent to run git status or other bash command
- Expected: Agent can use bash tool (follows global config)
- Verify: Commands execute

### Scenario 7: Other beads agents unchanged
- Manually invoke beads-task-agent
- Expected: Task agent cannot ask questions (no question tool)
- Verify: Task agent executes work without question tool

## Files to Check
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-planner-agent.md
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-task-agent.md (verify no question tool)
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-review-agent.md (verify no question tool)
- knowledge-base/agent/beads-verify-agent.md (verify no question tool)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 test scenarios pass
- [ ] Question tool works with single questions
- [ ] Question tool works with multiple questions
- [ ] Agent properly blocked from editing code
- [ ] Agent can use bd commands without issues
- [ ] Other beads agents don't have question tool
- [ ] No regressions observed

## Notes
Document any issues found during testing. If bugs discovered, create separate bug issues and link them with `bd dep add <bug-id> <this-task-id> --type discovered-from`.
close_reason: All 7 test scenarios passed. Question tool works correctly in beads-planner-agent with single/multiple questions, read-only enforcement verified, bd/bash commands work, other agents unchanged.

title: [beads-verify-agent] Bypasses explicit verification steps with read-only substitutes (github:#3)
description:
## Original GitHub Issue

**GitHub**: hk9890/opencode-coder#3
**Created**: 2026-01-05

## Problem

The `beads-verify-agent` ignored explicit verification steps in gate criteria and substituted safer/easier checks that don't actually verify functionality. This allowed a critical bug (session not starting) to pass verification and reach "complete" status.

## Expected Behavior

The gate had explicit verification steps:

```
## Verification Steps
1. cd to any project directory
2. Run `os` - should create session file and start kitty session
3. Run `os` again - should focus existing session (not create new)
4. Check ~/.config/kitty/sessions/ for session file
5. Restart kitty, run `os` - should use existing session file
6. Verify bin/occ and scripts/opencode/* are untouched
```

The verifier should have executed these steps and observed the actual behavior.

## Actual Behavior

The verifier substituted different checks:

| Gate Criterion | Required Action | What Verifier Did |
|----------------|-----------------|-------------------|
| `os` works end-to-end | Run `os`, verify window opens | Ran `os --help`, `os status`, `os list` (read-only) |
| Sessions detected/focused | Run `os` twice, verify focus | "Code review of `session_is_running()` function" |
| Manual testing confirms workflow | Actually test the workflow | Claimed testing without execution |

## Impact

- The `os` command doesn't actually start sessions (critical bug missed)
- Epic was marked complete when core functionality is broken
- User discovered the bug during manual testing after "verification passed"

## Root Causes

1. **Risk aversion**: Agent avoided side effects (spawning windows) by substituting read-only commands
2. **Misunderstanding verification**: Agent treated "verification" as "code review" rather than "functional testing"
3. **No accountability**: No mechanism to prove the agent actually ran the specified verification steps
4. **No evidence required**: Agent can claim "verified" without proof of execution

## Recommendations

1. Verify agent should be required to show command output as evidence
2. Gate criteria with "Run X" should not be substitutable with "Run X --help"
3. Consider adding a "verification log" that captures actual commands run
4. Code review should never substitute for functional testing of runtime behavior

---
*Imported from GitHub via /github/sync-issues*
close_reason: Fixed by adding Verification Integrity Rules to beads-verify-agent: No Substitution Rule, Evidence Requirement, UNVERIFIED status option

title: Planner must track tasks that need discussion before execution
description:
## Problem

When the orchestrator/planner creates tasks from imported issues or user discussions, some tasks are not ready for execution — they need further scoping, discussion, or user input before a tasker should touch them.

Currently the planner has no guidance on how to signal this. In the oc-fy9o epic, we had to manually set status=blocked and add a needs-discussion label to prevent .5 and .6 from showing up in bd ready. This was ad-hoc — the planning workflow should make this explicit.

## What needs to change

Update the opencode-coder skill (ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/) — specifically the planning references — to instruct the planner to:

1. Distinguish between 'ready to execute' and 'needs discussion' tasks at creation time.
2. Use a consistent mechanism in beads to track this. Options to evaluate:
   - status=blocked (current ad-hoc approach — but 'blocked' implies a dependency, not a discussion need)
   - A dedicated label like needs-discussion or needs-scoping
   - The defer flag (bd create --defer) to hide from bd ready until a date
   - A combination of the above
3. Document the chosen pattern in the planning references so future sessions follow it consistently.

## Scope

- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/planning.md (primary target)
- Potentially the orchestrator agent (ai-resources/agents/orchestrator.md) if it needs guidance too
- No TypeScript changes — this is purely AI resource layer.
close_reason: Implemented needs:discussion pattern across all agent files and planning reference

title: Improve /init skill discovery — only propose skills relevant to the repo
description:
## Problem

The /init command's skill discovery step proposes skills that don't make sense for the project. For example, it might recommend a Bitbucket skill for a GitHub-hosted repo, or suggest framework-specific skills for a project that doesn't use that framework.

This has been observed across multiple projects where /init was run. The skill selection feels generic rather than context-aware.

## Expected Behavior

Skill discovery should analyze the repo before proposing skills:
- Check git remote to determine hosting platform (GitHub vs GitLab vs Bitbucket) — only propose matching integration skills
- Check package.json / go.mod / Cargo.toml / etc. to determine tech stack — only propose relevant framework skills
- Check existing config (.opencode/, .github/, etc.) to detect what's already set up
- Filter out skills that are clearly irrelevant to the repo context

## Where to fix

- ai-resources/commands/init.md — the /init command template that drives skill discovery
- ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/ — if there's a planning or init reference that guides the discovery workflow
- Possibly the aimgr skill metadata — skills may need better metadata (e.g. 'requires: github' or 'tech: react') so the init command can filter intelligently

## Scope

This is an AI resource layer improvement — the /init command markdown needs smarter instructions for the agent to follow during discovery. May also need skill metadata conventions so filtering is possible.
close_reason: Superseded by epic oc-c8ur which implements the full solution: ProjectDetector + .coder/project.yaml + ai-resource-manager recommendation + init delegation

title: Add toast notification for beads not available
description:
## Description
Show a warning toast when beads functionality is expected but not available. Help users understand why beads features aren't working.

## Instructions
1. Modify `src/service/beads-service.ts`
2. Add a new method to check beads availability and show toast:
   - Check if `bd` CLI is installed (run `which bd` or similar)
   - Check if `.beads` directory exists
3. Show appropriate toast messages:
   - If `bd` not installed: "Beads CLI not found. Install with: npm install -g beads"
   - If `.beads` missing: "Beads not initialized for this project. Run: bd init"
   - If BOTH conditions pass: no toast (beads is working)
4. Call this check during plugin initialization (after beads detection)
5. Use `client.tui.showToast()` with `variant: "warning"`

## Files to Modify
- `src/service/beads-service.ts` - add toast notification logic
- `src/index.ts` - possibly call the toast check during init

## API Reference
```typescript
await client.tui.showToast({
  body: {
    title: "Beads Not Available",
    message: "Beads CLI not found. Install with: npm install -g beads",
    variant: "warning",
    duration: 5000
  }
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toast shown when `bd` CLI not installed
- [ ] Toast shown when `.beads` directory missing
- [ ] No toast when both conditions pass (beads working)
- [ ] Toast uses warning variant
- [ ] Toast message is helpful and actionable
close_reason: Added checkBeadsAvailability method to BeadsService. Shows toast when bd CLI not installed or .beads directory missing. Called during plugin init. Added tests for all scenarios.

title: Update status-health.md to use coder tool
description:
## Description
Replace hardcoded plugin paths and complex bash scripts in status-health.md with coder tool calls.

## Instructions

### 1. Update "Plugin Installation" section (lines 118-129)
Replace the hardcoded PLUGIN_PATH check:
```bash
PLUGIN_PATH="$HOME/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder"
if [ -d "$PLUGIN_PATH" ]; then
    ...
fi
```

With coder tool usage:
```markdown
**Using the coder tool (recommended):**
The AI assistant can use the `coder` tool to check plugin status:
- `coder("version")` - Returns plugin name and version
- `coder("plugin")` - Returns full plugin status including active/disabled state

**Manual check:**
```bash
# Check OpenCode config for installed plugins
cat ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json | grep -A5 '"plugin"'
```

### 2. Update "Version Checking" section (lines 239-260)
Replace hardcoded paths like:
```bash
cat ~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder/package.json | grep version
node -p "require('$HOME/.cache/opencode/node_modules/@hk9890/opencode-coder/package.json').version"
```

With coder tool documentation:
```markdown
### Plugin Version (via coder tool)
The AI assistant can query plugin version using the coder tool:
- `coder("version")` returns the plugin name and version (e.g., "@hk9890/opencode-coder v0.26.1")

### Manual Version Check
```bash
# Via OpenCode config
cat ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json | grep -A5 '"plugin"'
```

### 3. Update "Comprehensive Status Check" script (lines 97-189)
Simplify the bash script by:
- Removing the PLUGIN_PATH check (AI uses coder tool instead)
- Keep essential bd CLI and beads checks
- Add note that AI assistant uses `coder` tool for plugin-specific checks

### 4. Add new "Coder Tool Reference" section
Add after "Overview" section:
```markdown
## Using the Coder Tool

The AI assistant has access to a `coder` tool that provides real-time status information:

| Command | Description | Example Output |
|---------|-------------|----------------|
| `coder("version")` | Plugin version | `@hk9890/opencode-coder v0.26.1` |
| `coder("plugin")` | Plugin status | Shows name, version, active state, debug mode |
| `coder("beads")` | Beads status | Initialized, mode, hooks status, directory |
| `coder("logs")` | Log directory | Path, file count, latest log |

This replaces the need for hardcoded path checks in shell scripts.
```

title: End-to-end test bitbucket-pr scripts with PR 2639
description:
## Description
Test all scripts against the real Bitbucket instance using PR 2639 (PFS/copilot-skills).

## Instructions
1. Test bb-api.sh read functions:
   ```bash
   cd ai-resources/skills/bitbucket-pr
   export BITBUCKET_TOKEN='your_token'
   source scripts/bb-api.sh
   bb_get_pr PFS copilot-skills 2639
   bb_get_pr_changes PFS copilot-skills 2639
   bb_get_pr_comments PFS copilot-skills 2639
   bb_get_pr_tasks PFS copilot-skills 2639
   ```

2. Test create-or-update-pr.sh (dry-run only):
   ```bash
   ./scripts/create-or-update-pr.sh create --dry-run
   ```

3. Test review-pr.sh:
   ```bash
   # Fetch data
   ./scripts/review-pr.sh 2639 --dry-run
   # Test with sample feedback
   echo '{"summary": "Test review", "items": []}' | \
     ./scripts/review-pr.sh 2639 --dry-run
   ```

4. Test process-pr-feedback.sh:
   ```bash
   # Fetch feedback
   ./scripts/process-pr-feedback.sh 2639
   # Test with sample actions (dry-run)
   echo '{"resolve_tasks": [], "replies": []}' | \
     ./scripts/process-pr-feedback.sh 2639 --dry-run
   ```

5. Document and fix any issues found

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All bb-api.sh read functions tested and returning correct data
- [ ] create-or-update-pr.sh tested (dry-run at minimum)
- [ ] review-pr.sh tested with PR 2639 (fetches data correctly)
- [ ] process-pr-feedback.sh tested with PR 2639 (fetches feedback correctly)
- [ ] All errors fixed
- [ ] Scripts handle edge cases (missing token, invalid PR ID, etc.)
close_reason: All 7 tests passed. Found and fixed 3 bugs: (1) bb_get_pr_tasks used non-existent /pull-requests/{id}/tasks endpoint — fixed to use activities endpoint filtered by severity=BLOCKER (tasks are BLOCKER-severity comments in Bitbucket Server 9.x); (2) bb_create_task used non-existent /tasks endpoint — fixed to POST to /comments with severity=BLOCKER; (3) bb_resolve_task used non-existent /tasks/{id} endpoint — fixed to PUT /comments/{id} with state=RESOLVED and updated signature to take project/repo/pr_id/version; (4) review-pr.sh fetch crashed with 'Argument list too long' on 100-file PRs — fixed by using tempfile + --slurpfile instead of shell variable for diffs_obj; (5) review-pr.sh post --dry-run overwrote user-provided BB_PROJECT/BB_REPO by unconditionally calling bb_extract_repo_info — fixed to only auto-detect when not already set.

## Description

## Context
The beads detector currently accepts a config parameter to check for explicit `config.beads.enabled` override. Since we're removing this config option, the detector should only rely on auto-detection (.beads directory existence).

## Instructions

title: Migrate GitHub sync to bidirectional task-sync skill architecture
description:
## Description

Migrate the existing GitHub issue sync command (`knowledge-base/command/github/sync-issues.md`) to a skill-based architecture that supports bidirectional synchronization with multiple task systems (GitHub, Jira, etc.).

## Current State

- Single monolithic command: `knowledge-base/command/github/sync-issues.md` (~971 lines)
- GitHub-specific implementation (gh CLI commands embedded throughout)
- One-way sync with limited bidirectional support (import + closure sync)
- Hard to extend to other task systems (Jira, Linear, etc.)

## Goals

1. **Generic task-sync skill** - Abstract workflow that works with any task system
2. **Bidirectional sync** - Upload beads → external system, download external → beads
3. **Pluggable backends** - Easy to add new task systems (Jira, Linear, etc.)
4. **Modular architecture** - Separate conceptual workflow from implementation details
5. **Follow fix-documentation pattern** - Lean SKILL.md with references

## Architecture Vision

### Two-Layer Design

**Layer 1: `ai-resources/skills/task-sync/SKILL.md`**
- Generic conceptual workflow (system-agnostic)
- Decision tree for sync operations (import, export, bidirectional)
- References to backend-specific implementations
- ~100-150 lines (lean, like fix-documentation skill)

**Layer 2: Backend-specific skills**
- `ai-resources/skills/github-task-sync/SKILL.md` - GitHub implementation
- Future: `ai-resources/skills/jira-task-sync/SKILL.md` - Jira implementation
- Each contains system-specific commands and logic

### Sync Capabilities

**Import (External → Beads)**
- Fetch open issues from external system
- Create beads with `source:external` label
- Map priorities, labels, metadata
- Deduplicate (skip already imported)

**Export (Beads → External)**
- Push beads issues to external system
- Sync status changes (close, reopen)
- Sync comments, priority changes
- Track external IDs on beads

**Bidirectional**
- Combine import + export
- Conflict resolution strategy
- Source of truth decisions

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Generic task-sync skill created with clean architecture
- [ ] GitHub backend skill extracted from current command
- [ ] Bidirectional sync working (import + export)
- [ ] Original sync-issues.md command removed
- [ ] Documentation updated (README, AGENTS.md if needed)
- [ ] Tested with real GitHub repository

## Migration Strategy

Follow the fix-documentation migration pattern:
1. Create skill structure (SKILL.md + references/)
2. Extract and refactor existing logic
3. Test new skill implementation
4. Remove old command files
5. Update documentation

## Open Questions

- [ ] How to handle conflict resolution in bidirectional sync?
- [ ] Should we support multiple backends simultaneously? (e.g., sync to both GitHub and Jira)
- [ ] What metadata do we store on beads to track external sync? (labels? custom fields?)
- [ ] Do we need a sync log or history tracking?

---

**Related Work**: See commit 3967044 (Migrate fix commands to fix-documentation skill) for migration pattern reference.
close_reason: Epic COMPLETE. Successfully migrated 971-line sync-issues.md monolith to clean skill architecture. Created task-sync (orchestrator) and github-task-sync (backend) skills with full bidirectional sync, conflict detection, export capability (NEW), and pluggable architecture. All 11 tasks completed. Feature parity maintained, many enhancements added. Documentation updated. Old command removed. Ready for production use.

title: Auto-initialize aimgr on plugin startup
description:
## Description
Automatically detect and initialize aimgr when the plugin starts up. If aimgr is installed and no ai.package.yaml exists, initialize it and install the opencode-coder package.

## User Story
As a user with aimgr installed, I want the plugin to automatically set up the AI package ecosystem so I don't have to manually run aimgr commands.

## Requirements
1. On plugin startup, check if `ai.package.yaml` exists in the current directory
2. If not, check if `aimgr` command is available on PATH
3. If aimgr is available:
   - Run `aimgr init` in current directory
   - Check if `opencode-coder` package is available in aimgr repo
   - If available, run `aimgr install opencode-coder`
   - Display message to user about the automatic setup
4. Handle errors gracefully (don't block plugin startup)
5. Log all aimgr operations for debugging

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Plugin checks for ai.package.yaml on startup
- [ ] Plugin detects aimgr availability
- [ ] Plugin runs aimgr init when appropriate
- [ ] Plugin installs opencode-coder package when available
- [ ] User receives clear notification message
- [ ] Errors don't prevent plugin from loading
- [ ] All operations are logged

## Technical Notes
- This should run during plugin initialization (likely in src/index.ts)
- Use child_process to check for aimgr and run commands
- Make operations async to not block startup
- Consider caching the check result to avoid repeated checks
close_reason: Feature complete: AimgrService implemented, integrated, tested (18 tests), and documented. Auto-initialization runs on plugin startup, installs opencode-coder package when available, shows notifications, handles errors gracefully.

title: AGENTS.md stealth isolation — move to .coder/ and inject via plugin config hook
description:
## Problem

Current stealth mode places AGENTS.md at the project root and adds it to .git/info/exclude. This has a critical flaw:

- `.git/info/exclude` (like .gitignore) only works for **UNTRACKED** files
- If a project already has a committed AGENTS.md (tracked in git), the exclude entry does NOTHING
- Modifications to a tracked file show in `git status` and `git diff` — stealth is broken
- The only ways to hide changes to tracked files (`--skip-worktree`, `--assume-unchanged`) were rejected as dangerous

This was missed in the original implementation (epic oc-nbp0) because the init.md incorrectly claimed that .git/info/exclude would hide changes to a tracked AGENTS.md (line 284).

## Solution

**Always place AGENTS.md in `.coder/AGENTS.md` in stealth mode** — never at the project root.

The plugin injects `.coder/AGENTS.md` into OpenCode via the native `config` hook:

```typescript
// In the config hook
config: async (input) => {
  if (await fileExists('.coder/AGENTS.md')) {
    input.instructions = input.instructions || [];
    input.instructions.push('.coder/AGENTS.md');
  }
}
```

This uses OpenCode's built-in `instructions` mechanism — no experimental APIs.

### Why this works for all cases

**Case 1: Fresh project, no team AGENTS.md**
- We create `.coder/AGENTS.md` with full content (project overview, doc routing, beads workflow)
- Plugin injects it → OpenCode loads it as instructions
- No root AGENTS.md needed, no exclude entry needed

**Case 2: Project has a tracked AGENTS.md from the team**
- Team's AGENTS.md at root is loaded by OpenCode automatically (built-in convention)
- We create `.coder/AGENTS.md` with our additions (doc paths, skill refs, beads)
- Plugin injects it → OpenCode combines both
- Team's file untouched, our file invisible (under .coder/ which is excluded)

### What changes

1. **Plugin TypeScript**: Add `config` hook to inject `.coder/AGENTS.md` if it exists
2. **Exclude block**: Remove standalone `AGENTS.md` line — it's now under `.coder/` which is already excluded
3. **init.md**: Stealth mode creates `.coder/AGENTS.md` instead of root AGENTS.md
4. **agents-md-template.md**: Stealth output goes to `.coder/AGENTS.md`
5. **Supporting docs**: Update file paths and transition instructions

### Exclude block before vs after

**Before (5 entries):**
```
# opencode-coder stealth mode
.beads/
.opencode/
.coder/
ai.package.yaml
AGENTS.md
```

**After (4 entries):**
```
# opencode-coder stealth mode
.beads/
.opencode/
.coder/
ai.package.yaml
```

AGENTS.md is no longer needed — .coder/ already covers .coder/AGENTS.md.

title: Plugin: add config hook to inject .coder/AGENTS.md as instructions
init.md: stealth creates .coder/AGENTS.md, update exclude block to 4 entries
description:
## What

Update `ai-resources/commands/init.md` so stealth mode creates `.coder/AGENTS.md` instead of a root AGENTS.md, and reduce the exclude block from 5 to 4 entries.

## Why

The current init creates AGENTS.md at the project root and adds it to .git/info/exclude. But exclude only works for untracked files — if the project has a tracked AGENTS.md, stealth breaks. By moving to .coder/AGENTS.md, the file is covered by the existing .coder/ exclusion.

## Changes

### 1. Exclude block (line ~200-212)
Remove the `AGENTS.md` line from the stealth exclusion block:

**Before:**
```bash
cat >> .git/info/exclude << 'STEALTH'
# opencode-coder stealth mode
.beads/
.opencode/
.coder/
ai.package.yaml
AGENTS.md
STEALTH
```

**After:**
```bash
cat >> .git/info/exclude << 'STEALTH'
# opencode-coder stealth mode
.beads/
.opencode/
.coder/
ai.package.yaml
STEALTH
```

### 2. Stealth workspace creation (line ~195)
Already creates `.coder/docs`. No change needed — AGENTS.md creation is in Step 3.

### 3. Step 3 AGENTS.md path (lines 251-287)
The Step 3 description says AGENTS.md is created using the agents-md-template.md. Add a note that in stealth mode, the output path is `.coder/AGENTS.md` (not root).

**Update the Stealth Mode: Path Awareness section** to include AGENTS.md itself:

| Standard path | Stealth path |
|---|---|
| `AGENTS.md` | `.coder/AGENTS.md` |
| `docs/CODING.md` | `.coder/docs/CODING.md` |
| `docs/TESTING.md` | `.coder/docs/TESTING.md` |
| `docs/RELEASING.md` | `.coder/docs/RELEASING.md` |
| `docs/MONITORING.md` | `.coder/docs/MONITORING.md` |

### 4. Incorporating a Team AGENTS.md section (lines 276-287)
Rewrite to reflect the new approach:
- If team has a committed AGENTS.md (`git show HEAD:AGENTS.md`), read it for context
- Our .coder/AGENTS.md adds opencode-coder sections (skill refs, doc routing, beads workflow)
- The plugin injects .coder/AGENTS.md via config hook — OpenCode loads both
- Remove the incorrect claim that exclude hides changes to tracked files

### 5. Stealth-to-Team Transition (lines 290-310)
Update to reflect that AGENTS.md moves from .coder/ to root:
```bash
# Move AGENTS.md to root (or merge with team's if one exists)
cp .coder/AGENTS.md ./AGENTS.md
# Copy docs to standard locations
cp -r .coder/docs/ ./docs/
# Update all .coder/docs/ paths to docs/ in AGENTS.md
# Remove stealth block from .git/info/exclude
# Clean up: rm -rf .coder/
```

### 6. Detection logic (lines 153-169)
The re-run detection checks for the marker in .git/info/exclude — this stays the same. But the 'fully configured in team mode' check (line 165) currently checks for AGENTS.md at root. In stealth mode, AGENTS.md is at .coder/AGENTS.md, so the re-run flow handles this correctly (marker found → stealth active → skip mode question).

## Acceptance

- Exclude block has 4 entries (no standalone AGENTS.md)
- Stealth mode path table includes AGENTS.md row
- Team AGENTS.md incorporation section is accurate (no false claims about exclude)
- Transition section moves .coder/AGENTS.md to root
- Detection logic is consistent

title: agents-md-template.md: stealth output to .coder/AGENTS.md
description:
## What

Update `ai-resources/skills/opencode-coder/references/agents-md-template.md` so that in stealth mode, the generated AGENTS.md is written to `.coder/AGENTS.md` instead of the project root.

## Why

The template generates AGENTS.md content and writes it to a file. In stealth mode, the output path must be `.coder/AGENTS.md` to match the new approach where everything stealth lives under `.coder/`.

## Changes

### 1. Standard File Convention table (line 13-22)
Add a note about AGENTS.md output path:

> **Stealth mode**: AGENTS.md itself is written to `.coder/AGENTS.md` (not the project root). All `docs/` paths become `.coder/docs/`. See [Mode Detection](#mode-detection).

### 2. Mode Detection section (lines 30-44)
Update the description to clarify that stealth mode affects the OUTPUT path too:

- **Stealth mode** (marker found): AGENTS.md is written to `.coder/AGENTS.md`. Documentation files live under `.coder/docs/`.
- **Team mode** (marker not found): AGENTS.md is written to project root. Documentation files live under `docs/`.

Update the `{docs}` convention to also include `{agents_md}`:
- `{agents_md}` = `.coder/AGENTS.md` in stealth mode, `AGENTS.md` in team mode
- `{docs}` = `.coder/docs/` in stealth mode, `docs/` in team mode

### 3. Step 1 Gather Context (line 49-68)
The explore prompt asks to check for AGENTS.md. Add `.coder/AGENTS.md` to the search:
> Also check for `.coder/AGENTS.md` (stealth mode location)

### 4. Step 3 Migration Decision (lines 88-119)
Stealth migration targets already reference `.coder/docs/`. No change needed.

### 5. Step 5 Generate AGENTS.md (lines 131-257)
The path convention note should be updated:
> **Output path**: Write the generated file to `{agents_md}` — `.coder/AGENTS.md` in stealth mode, `AGENTS.md` (project root) in team mode.

### 6. Step 6 Verify (lines 259-273)
Add verification item:
- [ ] **Stealth mode only**: AGENTS.md written to `.coder/AGENTS.md`, NOT to project root

### 7. Updating an Existing AGENTS.md (lines 277-290)
Add note: when updating in stealth mode, the file being updated is `.coder/AGENTS.md`.

### 8. Handle team AGENTS.md coexistence
Add a new section or note about what happens when a team AGENTS.md exists at root AND we're in stealth mode:
- Read the team AGENTS.md (`git show HEAD:AGENTS.md` or directly from root if it's tracked)
- Our `.coder/AGENTS.md` does NOT need to duplicate the team's content
- Focus on adding: opencode-coder doc routing (pointing to .coder/docs/), skill references, beads workflow
- The plugin injects our file alongside the team's → OpenCode sees both

## Acceptance

- Mode detection section mentions output path for AGENTS.md
- Generate step writes to .coder/AGENTS.md in stealth mode
- Verify step checks output path
- Update section references correct path
- Coexistence with team AGENTS.md is documented

title: Supporting docs: update-agent-md, installation-setup, troubleshooting

title: Gate: verify stealth AGENTS.md isolation end-to-end
description:
## Verification

Verify the complete AGENTS.md stealth isolation works end-to-end.

### Plugin verification
- [ ] `src/index.ts` has a `config` hook that adds `.coder/AGENTS.md` to `instructions` when the file exists
- [ ] When `.coder/AGENTS.md` does not exist, the hook is a no-op
- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
- [ ] Tests pass: `bun test`

### Agent instruction verification
- [ ] init.md stealth exclude block has 4 entries (no standalone AGENTS.md)
- [ ] init.md stealth path table includes AGENTS.md → .coder/AGENTS.md mapping
- [ ] init.md team AGENTS.md incorporation section has no false claims about exclude hiding tracked files
- [ ] init.md transition section moves .coder/AGENTS.md to root
- [ ] agents-md-template.md mode detection mentions output path
- [ ] agents-md-template.md stealth verify checklist includes output path check
- [ ] update-agent-md.md references correct stealth output path
- [ ] installation-setup.md file structure shows .coder/AGENTS.md
- [ ] installation-setup.md exclude block has 4 entries
- [ ] troubleshooting-patterns.md is consistent

### Consistency verification
- [ ] Search entire codebase for the old 5-entry exclude block — should find NONE
- [ ] Search for 'AGENTS.md' in exclude context — should only appear in transition docs (moving from .coder/ to root)
- [ ] No file references root AGENTS.md as the stealth location
- [ ] All stealth file references are under .coder/
notes: Verified: all criteria met. Plugin config hook correctly checks for .coder/AGENTS.md, pushes relative path, no-op when absent, uses worktree base. Build succeeds (161 modules), 65/65 tests pass. init.md has 4-entry stealth block, path awareness table, correct transition and coexistence guidance. agents-md-template.md has {agents_md} variable, stealth path descriptions, Step 5 path note, Step 6 output path check, Coexistence section with plugin config hook explanation. update-agent-md.md, installation-setup.md, and troubleshooting-patterns.md all correctly reference .coder/AGENTS.md for stealth. No 5-entry exclude blocks found anywhere. One unrelated bug filed as oc-zxdr.7.

title: Bug: init.md re-run flow step 5 incorrectly says 'Update AGENTS.md at the project root' instead of '.coder/AGENTS.md' in stealth mode context
fix-documentation skill has no stealth mode awareness for AGENTS.md path
description:
The fix-documentation skill files in ai-resources/skills/fix-documentation/ all reference AGENTS.md without stealth-mode awareness:

- fix-agents-md.md — gives instructions to fix AGENTS.md but assumes project root
- fix-project-documentation.md — lists AGENTS.md as a core file, no stealth path
- project-documentation-checklist.md — references AGENTS.md paths/commands/quality, no stealth variant
- SKILL.md — offers fix workflow with no stealth awareness

If someone runs the fix-documentation skill in stealth mode, it will look for/modify root AGENTS.md instead of .coder/AGENTS.md.

Found by reviewer during oc-zxdr verification.
